title.zh: 卷六 孝順孝沖孝質帝紀
title.en: Volume 6: Annals of Emperor Xiaoshun; Emperor Xiaochong; Emperor Xiaozhi
title.raw: 卷六 孝順孝沖孝質帝紀 Volume 6: Annals of Emperor Xiaoshun; Emperor Xiaochong; Emperor Xiaozhi

sentences.zh: 十二月甲申，以少府河南陶敦為司空。

translations.literal: On the day jiashen of the twelfth month, Tao Dun of Henan, Superintendent of the Household, was appointed Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiashen day of the twelfth month, Tao Dun of Henan, who had served as Superintendent of the Household, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 令郡國守、相視事未滿歲者，一切得舉孝廉吏。

translations.literal: It was ordered that commandery and kingdom governors and chancellors who had held office less than one full year might in every case recommend filial and incorrupt officials.
translations.idiomatic: Governors of commanderies and chancellors of kingdoms who had been in post for less than a year were now allowed across the board to nominate candidates for the filial-and-incorrupt recommendation.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，尚書奏請下有司，收還延光三年九月丁酉以皇太子為濟陰王詔書。

translations.literal: On the day guimao, the Masters of Writing memorialized, requesting that the relevant offices be ordered to recall the edict of the ninth month, day dingyou, of the third year of Yanguang, which had made the heir apparent Prince of Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao, the Masters of Writing asked that orders go down to the appropriate agencies to withdraw the edict issued on dingyou in the ninth month of the third Yanguang year that had demoted the heir apparent to Prince of Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The throne approved the memorial.

sentences.zh: 京師大疫。

translations.literal: In the capital there was a great epidemic.
translations.idiomatic: A severe epidemic struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，詔公卿、郡守、國相，舉賢良方正、能直言極諫之士各一人。

translations.literal: On the day xinhai, an edict ordered the dukes and ministers, commandery governors, and kingdom chancellors each to recommend one worthy and good man of upright character who could speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai, an edict instructed the high ministers, commandery governors, and kingdom chancellors each to nominate one candidate: a worthy and upright man able to speak plainly and remonstrate forcefully.

sentences.zh: 尚書令以下從輦幸南宮者，皆增秩賜布各有差。

translations.literal: Those from the Prefect of the Masters of Writing downward who had followed the imperial carriage in attending the Southern Palace were all promoted in rank and granted cloth in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: Every official from the Prefect of the Masters of Writing on down who had accompanied the imperial carriage to the Southern Palace received a promotion in rank and a grant of cloth, the amounts varying by station.

sentences.zh: 永建元年春正月甲寅，詔曰：『先帝聖德，享祚未永，早弃鴻烈。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongjian, spring, first month, day jiayin, an edict said: "The late emperor's sagely virtue did not long enjoy the throne; he early abandoned his great enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of the first Yongjian year, on jiayin in the first month, an edict declared: "Our late father possessed sagely virtue, yet his allotted reign was not long; he left his grand legacy too soon.

sentences.zh: 奸慝緣間，人庶怨讟，上干和氣，疫癘為災。

translations.literal: Wicked men took advantage of the interval; the people nursed grievances and slander; they offended the harmonious qi, and pestilence became disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Villains exploited the breach; common folk muttered resentment and blame; harmony between Heaven and man was disturbed, and epidemic disease followed as calamity.

sentences.zh: 朕奉承大業，未能寧濟。

translations.literal: We have received the great enterprise but have not been able to bring peace and succor.
translations.idiomatic: We have inherited the great charge yet have failed to restore stability and relief to the realm.

sentences.zh: 蓋至理之本，稽弘德惠，蕩滌宿惡，與人更始。

translations.literal: For the root of perfect order lies in examining and extending virtue and kindness, washing away long-standing evil, and giving the people a fresh beginning.
translations.idiomatic: The foundation of true order is to review and spread generous virtue, scour away entrenched wrongdoing, and let all begin anew.

sentences.zh: 其大赦天下。

translations.literal: Let there be a great amnesty of the empire.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore We grant a general amnesty throughout the empire.

sentences.zh: 賜男子爵，人二級，為父後、三老、孝悌、力田人三級，流民欲自占者一級；

translations.literal: Noble rank was bestowed on adult males, two grades each; on heirs to fathers, elders of three villages, those exemplary in filial piety and fraternal duty, and promoters of agriculture, three grades each; on vagrants who wished to register themselves, one grade;
translations.idiomatic: Adult males were granted two steps of noble rank; heirs, village elders, models of filial piety and brotherly harmony, and champions of farming received three steps each; displaced persons who chose to register locally received one step;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛；

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the seriously infirm, and the destitute who could not support themselves, grain, five hu each;
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely disabled, and the poor unable to survive on their own were given grain, five hu per person;

sentences.zh: 貞婦帛，人三匹。

translations.literal: to chaste wives, silk, three bolts each.
translations.idiomatic: virtuous widows received three bolts of silk each.

sentences.zh: 坐法當徙，勿徙；

translations.literal: Those sentenced by law to banishment were not to be banished;
translations.idiomatic: Those liable to banishment under the law were not to be sent into exile;

sentences.zh: 亡徒當傳，勿傳。

translations.literal: fugitive convicts who should have been forwarded were not to be forwarded.
translations.idiomatic: escaped laborers who would normally be transferred under escort were not to be transferred.

sentences.zh: 宗室以罪絕，皆復屬籍。

translations.literal: Imperial clansmen whose lines had been cut off for crime were all restored to the registers.
translations.idiomatic: Members of the imperial clan whose houses had been struck from the rolls for crimes were all readmitted to clan registration.

sentences.zh: 其與閻顯、江京等交通者，悉勿考。

translations.literal: As for those who had dealings with Yan Xian, Jiang Jing, and the like, let none be investigated.
translations.idiomatic: Those who had associated with Yan Xian, Jiang Jing, and their circle were not to be prosecuted further.

sentences.zh: 勉修厥職，以康我民。』

translations.literal: Strive to fulfill your duties, to bring peace to Our people."
translations.idiomatic: Apply yourselves to your offices, that Our people may thrive."

sentences.zh: 辛未，皇太后閻氏崩。

translations.literal: On the day xinwei, Empress Dowager of the Yan clan died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei the empress dowager of the Yan clan died.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，太傅馮石、太尉劉熹、司徒李郃免。

translations.literal: On the day xinsi, Grand Tutor Feng Shi, Grand Commandant Liu Xi, and Minister of Education Li He were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi Grand Tutor Feng Shi, Grand Commandant Liu Xi, and Minister of Education Li He were removed from office.

sentences.zh: 二月甲申，葬安思皇后。

translations.literal: On the day jiashen of the second month, Empress Consort Ansi was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the second month the late Empress Ansi was interred.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，太常桓焉為太傅；

translations.literal: On the day bingxu, Huan Yan, Minister of Ceremonies, became Grand Tutor;
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu Huan Yan, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Grand Tutor;

sentences.zh: 大鴻臚朱寵為太尉，參錄尚書事；

translations.literal: Zhu Chong, Grand Herald, became Grand Commandant and participated in supervising the Masters of Writing;
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Chong, Grand Herald, became Grand Commandant with concurrent responsibility for the Masters of Writing;

sentences.zh: 長樂少府九江朱倀為司徒。

translations.literal: Zhu Chang of Jiujiang, Junior Steward of the Eternal Joy Palace, became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Chang of Jiujiang, Junior Steward of the Eternal Joy Palace, was made Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 賜百官隨輦宿衛及拜除者布各有差。

translations.literal: The hundred officials who had accompanied the night watch of the carriage and those who had received appointment were granted cloth in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: Officials who had ridden with the carriage on night guard and those newly appointed received grants of cloth in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 隴西鐘羌叛，護羌校尉馬賢討破之。

translations.literal: The Zhong Qiang of Longxi rebelled; the Colonel Protecting the Qiang, Ma Xian, attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhong Qiang of Longxi rose in revolt; Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xian campaigned against them and broke their force.

sentences.zh: 夏五月丁丑，詔幽、幷、涼州刺史，使各實二千石以下至黃綬，年老劣弱不任軍事者，上名。

translations.literal: In summer, fifth month, day dingchou, an edict ordered the governors of You, Bing, and Liang each to verify those from two thousand piculs downward to the yellow ribbon who were old, weak, or unfit for military affairs, and submit their names.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on dingchou in the fifth month, edicts went to the governors of You, Bing, and Liang: each was to canvass officials from two thousand piculs down to yellow-ribbon rank who were aged, feeble, or unfit for war, and report their names.

sentences.zh: 嚴勑障塞，繕設屯備，立秋之後，簡習戎馬。

translations.literal: Barrier passes were strictly ordered to be repaired and garrison preparations set up; after the Beginning of Autumn, troops and horses were to be drilled and exercised.
translations.idiomatic: Frontier barriers were to be strengthened, garrison works readied, and after the qi node Beginning of Autumn troops were to drill arms and horses.

sentences.zh: 六月己亥，封濟南王錯子顯為濟南王。

translations.literal: On the day jihai of the sixth month, Xian, son of Prince Xian of Jinan, was enfeoffed as Prince of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: On jihai in the sixth month Xian, son of the late Prince of Jinan, was invested as Prince of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚午，衛尉來歷為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day gengwu, Lai Li, Commandant of the Guards, became General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on gengwu in the seventh month, Commandant of the Guards Lai Li was appointed General of Chariots and Cavalry.

sentences.zh: 八月，鮮卑寇代郡，代郡太守李超戰歿。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, the Xianbei raided Dai Commandery; Commandery Governor Li Chao died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Xianbei horsemen invaded Dai Commandery; Governor Li Chao fell in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 九月辛亥，初令三公、尚書入奏事。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, day xinhai, for the first time it was ordered that the Three Dukes and the Masters of Writing should enter to memorialize on affairs.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the ninth month a new rule required the Three Dukes and the Masters of Writing to attend court to present memorials on state business.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛巳，詔減死罪以下徙邊；

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day xinsi, an edict ordered reduction of death sentences and below to banishment on the frontier;
translations.idiomatic: In winter, on xinsi in the tenth month, an edict commuted capital crimes and lesser offenses to exile on the frontier;

sentences.zh: 其亡命贖，各有差。

translations.literal: for fugitives, ransom was allowed, in differing degrees.
translations.idiomatic: fugitives from the law might redeem themselves under a graded schedule.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，司空陶敦免。

translations.literal: On the day dinghai, Minister of Works Tao Dun was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai Minister of Works Tao Dun was removed.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑犯邊。

translations.literal: The Xianbei violated the border.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders struck the frontier.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，遣黎陽營兵出屯中山北界。

translations.literal: On the day gengyin, troops of the Liyang camp were sent out to encamp on the northern border of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin soldiers of the Liyang camp were dispatched to garrison the northern marches of Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 告幽州刺史，其令緣邊郡增置步兵，列屯塞下。

translations.literal: The Governor of You was notified to order the commanderies along the border each to add foot soldiers and array garrisons below the passes.
translations.idiomatic: The Governor of You was instructed to have frontier commanderies raise additional infantry and line camps along the wall.

sentences.zh: 調五營弩師，郡舉五人，令教習戰射。

translations.literal: Crossbow specialists of the Five Camps were levied, five men per commandery, to be ordered to teach drill in combat archery.
translations.idiomatic: Crossbow instructors from the Five Camps were conscripted—five from each commandery—to train troops in mounted and foot archery.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，廷尉張皓為司空。

translations.literal: On the day renyin, Zhang Hao, Minister of Justice, became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin Zhang Hao, Minister of Justice, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 甲辰，詔以疫癘水潦，令人半輸今年田租；

translations.literal: On the day jiachen, an edict, because of pestilence and flood, ordered people to pay only half this year's field rent;
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen an edict cited plague and inundation and allowed the people to remit only half of the year's land tax;

sentences.zh: 傷害什四以上，勿收責；

translations.literal: where damage reached four-tenths or more, collection was not to be pressed;
translations.idiomatic: where crop loss was forty percent or higher, no collection was to be enforced;

sentences.zh: 不滿者，以實除之。

translations.literal: where it did not reach that, deductions were to be made according to the actual harm.
translations.idiomatic: where loss fell short of that threshold, abatements were to match the verified damage.

sentences.zh: 十二月辛巳，賜王、主、貴人、公卿以下布各有差。

translations.literal: On the day xinsi of the twelfth month, kings, princesses, noble ladies, and from dukes and ministers downward were granted cloth in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the twelfth month princes, princesses, noble consorts, and all officials from dukes down received graded gifts of cloth.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月戊申，樂安王鴻來朝。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month, day wushen, Prince Hong of Le'an came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, on wushen in the first spring month, the Prince of Le'an, Hong, attended court.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，常山王章薨。

translations.literal: On the day dingmao, Prince Zhang of Changshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao Prince Zhang of Changshan died.

sentences.zh: 二月，鮮卑寇遼東、玄菟。

translations.literal: In the second month, the Xianbei raided Liaodong and Xuantu.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Xianbei bands attacked Liaodong and Xuantu.

sentences.zh: 甲辰，詔稟貸荊、豫、兗、冀四州流冗貧人，所在安業之；

translations.literal: On the day jiachen, an edict ordered grain relief loans to the drifting poor of Jing, Yu, Yan, and Ji, that they be settled in productive work where they were;
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen an edict authorized grain loans to indigent refugees in Jing, Yu, Yan, and Ji so they could settle and work where they stood;

sentences.zh: 疾病致醫藥。

translations.literal: the sick were to be given medicine.
translations.idiomatic: those who fell ill were to receive medical care.

sentences.zh: 護烏桓校尉耿曄率南單于擊鮮卑，破之。

translations.literal: Geng Ye, Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan, led the Southern Chanyu to strike the Xianbei and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan Geng Ye, at the head of the Southern Chanyu, attacked the Xianbei and routed them.

sentences.zh: 三月，旱，遣使者錄囚徒。

translations.literal: In the third month there was drought; envoys were sent to review prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: The third month brought drought; envoys were dispatched to review cases in the jails.

sentences.zh: 疏勒國遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The state of Shule sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Shule sent a mission bearing tribute.

sentences.zh: 夏六月乙酉，追尊謚皇妣李氏為恭愍皇后，葬於恭北陵。

translations.literal: In summer, sixth month, day yiyou, the late Empress Dowager Li was posthumously honored with the title Empress Gongmin and buried at Gongbei Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on yiyou in the sixth month, Our birth mother Lady Li was given the posthumous title Empress Gongmin and laid to rest at the northern Gong mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 西域長史班勇、敦煌太守張朗討焉耆、尉犁、危須三國，破之；

translations.literal: Ban Yong, Chief Clerk of the Western Regions, and Zhang Lang, Governor of Dunhuang, campaigned against the three states of Yanqi, Weili, and Weixu and defeated them;
translations.idiomatic: Chief Clerk of the Western Regions Ban Yong and Dunhuang Governor Zhang Lang attacked Yanqi, Weili, and Weixu and crushed them;

sentences.zh: 並遣子貢獻。

translations.literal: they also sent sons with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: each state then sent a hostage son with offerings of tribute.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲戌朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day jiaxu, first of the month, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn, seventh month: the new moon fell on jiaxu and a solar eclipse was observed.

sentences.zh: 壬午，太尉朱寵、司徒朱倀罷。

translations.literal: On the day renwu, Grand Commandant Zhu Chong and Minister of Education Zhu Chang were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu Grand Commandant Zhu Chong and Minister of Education Zhu Chang were removed from office.

sentences.zh: 庚子，太常劉光為太尉，錄尚書事；

translations.literal: On the day gengzi, Liu Guang, Minister of Ceremonies, became Grand Commandant and supervised the Masters of Writing;
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi Liu Guang, Minister of Ceremonies, became Grand Commandant with charge of the Masters of Writing;

sentences.zh: 光祿勳許敬為司徒。

translations.literal: Xu Jing, Supervisor of the Household to the Heir Apparent, became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Supervisor of the Household Xu Jing was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，下邳王成薨。

translations.literal: On the day xinchou, Prince Cheng of Xiapi died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou Prince Cheng of Xiapi died.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月丙子，京師地震，漢陽地陷裂。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, first month, day bingzi, the capital was shaken by an earthquake; the earth in Hanyang subsided and split.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year, on bingzi in the first spring month, an earthquake rocked the capital; the ground in Hanyang sank and cracked open.

sentences.zh: 甲午，詔實覈傷害者，賜年七歲以上錢，人二千；

translations.literal: On the day jiawu, an edict ordered verification of those injured or killed and granted cash, two thousand per person, to those aged seven and above;
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu an edict required a tally of casualties and awarded two thousand cash to every victim aged seven or older;

sentences.zh: 一家被害，郡縣為收斂。

translations.literal: where a whole household had perished, the commandery or county was to bury them.
translations.idiomatic: where an entire household had been lost, the local commandery or county was to arrange the burials.

sentences.zh: 乙未，詔勿收漢陽今年田租、口賦。

translations.literal: On the day yiwei, an edict ordered that this year's field rent and poll tax for Hanyang not be collected.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei an edict waived Hanyang's land rent and poll levy for the year.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丁酉，茂陵園寢灾，帝縞素避正殿。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day dingyou, the park lodges at Mao Mausoleum caught fire; the emperor wore plain hemp and withdrew from the main hall.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on dingyou in the seventh month, fire destroyed the resting halls at Emperor Wu's Mao mausoleum; the emperor donned mourning white and vacated the main audience hall.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，使太常王龔持節告祠茂陵。

translations.literal: On the day xinhai, Minister of Ceremonies Wang Gong, bearing credentials, announced sacrifice at Mao Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai Minister of Ceremonies Wang Gong was sent with imperial staff to report the calamity at Mao and perform the rites.

sentences.zh: 九月，鮮卑寇漁陽。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the Xianbei raided Yuyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Xianbei raiders struck Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月己亥，太傅桓焉免。

translations.literal: In winter, twelfth month, day jihai, Grand Tutor Huan Yan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, on jihai in the twelfth month, Grand Tutor Huan Yan was removed.

sentences.zh: 是歲，車騎將軍來歷罷。

translations.literal: That year, General of Chariots and Cavalry Lai Li was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: The same year General of Chariots and Cavalry Lai Li was relieved of command.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月丙寅，詔曰：『朕托王公之上，涉道日寡，政失厥中，陰陽氣隔，寇盜肆暴，庶獄彌繁，憂悴永歎，疢如疾首。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, first month, day bingyin, an edict said: "We stand above the dukes and kings; Our path is shallow day by day; government has missed the mean; yin and yang are blocked apart; bandits and robbers run riot; lawsuits multiply endlessly; We sigh in sorrow and care as if sick at the brow.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year, on bingyin in the first spring month, an edict read: "We are set above kings and dukes, yet Our course grows ever shorter of virtue; policy has strayed from the middle way; yin and yang fail to communicate; brigandage rages; litigation piles up without end; We ache with worry like a pounding in the skull.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「君子如祉，亂庶遄已。

translations.literal: The 《Classic of Poetry》 says: 'When the lord is like a blessing, disorder will soon cease.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Classic of Poetry》 says, 'When the ruler becomes a source of blessing, rebellion will quickly end.'

sentences.zh: 」三朝之會，朔旦立春，嘉與海內洗心自新。

translations.literal: At the meeting of the three courts, the new moon and Beginning of Spring coincide; We rejoice with all within the seas to wash the heart and make a new beginning.
translations.idiomatic: The great court assembly falls on the new moon when spring opens; We would have everyone under Heaven cleanse the heart and begin again.

sentences.zh: 其赦天下。

translations.literal: Let the empire be amnestied.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore We proclaim a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 從甲寅赦令已來復秩屬籍，三年正月已來還贖。

translations.literal: From the jiayin amnesty order, restoration of rank and household registration, and from the first month of the third year, return of ransom payments.
translations.idiomatic: Those reinstated in rank and registers since the jiayin amnesty, and ransoms paid back since the first month of the third year, are confirmed.

sentences.zh: 其閻顯、江京等知識婚姻禁錮，一原除之。

translations.literal: As for the proscription on associates and affines of Yan Xian, Jiang Jing, and the like, let it all be remitted and removed.
translations.idiomatic: Proscriptions binding kin and marriage allies of Yan Xian, Jiang Jing, and their faction are lifted altogether.

sentences.zh: 務崇寬和，敬順時令，遵典去苛，以稱朕意。』

translations.literal: Strive for breadth and harmony, respect the seasons of the calendar, follow the canons and cast off harshness, to match Our mind."
translations.idiomatic: Seek leniency and accord, honor the seasons, hew to precedent and shed cruelty, that Our intent may be satisfied."

sentences.zh: 丙子，帝加元服。

translations.literal: On the day bingzi, the emperor assumed the cap of adulthood.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi the emperor underwent the capping ceremony.

sentences.zh: 賜王、主、貴人、公卿以下金、帛各有差。

translations.literal: Kings, princesses, noble ladies, and from dukes and ministers downward were granted gold and silk in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: Princes, princesses, noble consorts, and officials from dukes down received gifts of gold and silk in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 賜男子爵及流民欲占者人一級。

translations.literal: Noble rank was bestowed on adult males and on vagrants who wished to register, one grade each.
translations.idiomatic: Every adult male and every vagrant who chose to register locally received one step of noble rank.

sentences.zh: 為父後、三老、孝悌、力田人二級；

translations.literal: Heirs to fathers, elders of three villages, those exemplary in filial piety and fraternal duty, and promoters of agriculture received two grades each;
translations.idiomatic: Heirs, village elders, models of filial piety and brotherly duty, and champions of farming received two steps each;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存帛，人一匹。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the seriously infirm, and the destitute who could not support themselves, silk, one bolt each.
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely disabled, and the utterly destitute received one bolt of silk each.

sentences.zh: 二月戊戌，詔以民入山鑿石，發泄藏氣，勑有司檢察所當禁絕，如建武、永平故事。

translations.literal: On the day wuxu of the second month, an edict, because the people entered the mountains to quarry stone and thereby vented the stored qi, ordered the responsible offices to inspect what ought to be forbidden and stopped, according to the precedents of Jianwu and Yongping.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu in the second month an edict noted that quarrying stone in the hills released pent earth-energy; the relevant bureaus were to investigate closures and prohibitions on the model of the Jianwu and Yongping reigns.

sentences.zh: 夏五月壬辰，詔曰：『海內頗有灾異，朝廷修政，太官減膳，珍玩不御。

translations.literal: In summer, fifth month, day renchen, an edict said: "Within the seas there have been many disasters and prodigies; the court has cultivated government; the imperial kitchen has reduced meals; rare treasures are not used.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on renchen in the fifth month, an edict stated: "The realm has seen repeated omens and calamities; the court is reforming its rule; the palace kitchen has cut its fare; curios and luxuries are set aside.

sentences.zh: 而桂陽太守文礱，不惟竭忠，宣暢本朝，而遠獻大珠，以求幸媚，今封以還之。』

translations.literal: Yet Governor of Guiyang Wen Long, instead of exhausting loyalty and clarifying the court's intent from afar, presented great pearls to seek favor and flattery; now they are sealed and returned to him."
translations.idiomatic: Yet Wen Long, governor of Guiyang, instead of devoting himself to loyal service and voicing the court's will, sent up great pearls from afar to curry favor; We now seal them and send them back."

sentences.zh: 五州雨水。

translations.literal: Five commanderies were afflicted by rain and flood.
translations.idiomatic: Five provinces were inundated by rain.

sentences.zh: 秋八月庚子，遣使實覈死亡，收斂稟賜。

translations.literal: In autumn, eighth month, day gengzi, envoys were sent to verify the dead, gather the remains, and grant relief.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on gengzi in the eighth month, envoys were dispatched to verify deaths, collect bodies for burial, and issue relief grain.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，太尉劉光、司空張皓免。

translations.literal: On the day dingsi, Grand Commandant Liu Guang and Minister of Works Zhang Hao were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi Grand Commandant Liu Guang and Minister of Works Zhang Hao were removed.

sentences.zh: 九月，復安定、北地、上郡歸舊土。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Anding, Beidi, and Shang commanderies were restored to their old territories.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the people of Anding, Beidi, and Shang were allowed to return to their former lands.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，大鴻臚龐參為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On the day guiyou, Pang Can, Grand Herald, became Grand Commandant and supervised the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou Pang Can, Grand Herald, was appointed Grand Commandant with oversight of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 太常王龔為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Wang Gong became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Ceremonies Wang Gong became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月庚辰，司徒許敬免。

translations.literal: In winter, eleventh month, day gengchen, Minister of Education Xu Jing was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, on gengchen in the eleventh month, Minister of Education Xu Jing was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇朔方。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided Shuofang.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei horsemen invaded Shuofang.

sentences.zh: 十二月乙卯，宗正劉崎為司徒。

translations.literal: On the day yimao of the twelfth month, Liu Qi, Director of the Imperial Clan, became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao in the twelfth month Liu Qi, Director of the Imperial Clan, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 是歲，分會稽為吳郡。

translations.literal: That year, Kuaiji was divided and Wu Commandery was established.
translations.idiomatic: The same year Kuaiji was split and Wu Commandery carved out.

sentences.zh: 拘彌國遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: The state of Jumi sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Jumi sent envoys bearing tribute.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月，疏勒王遣侍子，及大宛、莎車王皆奉使貢獻。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, spring, first month, the king of Shule sent a hostage son, and the kings of Dayuan and Shache each sent missions with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year's first spring month the king of Shule sent his heir as hostage, while the kings of Dayuan and Shache each dispatched embassies with tribute.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，京師旱。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, the capital suffered drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth summer month drought gripped the capital.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，詔郡國貧人被灾者，勿收責今年過更。

translations.literal: On the day xinsi, an edict ordered that commanderies and kingdoms not collect from the poor who had suffered disaster this year's surcharge for alternate service.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi an edict directed commanderies and kingdoms not to demand from disaster-stricken poor the year's levy for hired substitute corvée, the guogeng surcharge.

sentences.zh: 京師及郡國十二蝗。

translations.literal: Locusts afflicted the capital and twelve commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Swarms of locusts devastated the capital and twelve commanderies.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丙辰，詔郡國中都官死罪繫囚皆減罪一等，詣北地、上郡、安定戍。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day bingchen, an edict ordered that capital prisoners in commanderies and kingdoms sentenced to death all have their crimes reduced one grade and be sent to garrison Beidi, Shang, and Anding.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the tenth winter month an edict commuted capital convicts held in commandery and kingdom jails by one degree of severity and reassigned them to frontier garrison duty in Beidi, Shang, and Anding.

sentences.zh: 乙亥，定遠侯班始坐殺其妻陰城公主，腰斬，同產皆弃市。

translations.literal: On the day yihai, Marquis Dingyuan Ban Shi, for having killed his wife Princess Yincheng, was cut in two at the waist; his full siblings were all executed in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai Marquis Dingyuan Ban Shi was bisected at the waist for murdering his wife, Princess Yincheng; every sibling born of the same parents was dragged to the public market and put to death.

sentences.zh: 六年春二月庚午，河間王開薨。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, spring, second month, day gengwu, Prince Kai of Hejian died.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, on gengwu in the second spring month, Prince Kai of Hejian died.

sentences.zh: 三月辛亥，復伊吾屯田，復置伊吾司馬一人。

translations.literal: In the third month, day xinhai, the Yiwu military colony was restored, and one Major of Yiwu was again appointed.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the third month the Yiwu garrison farms were reinstated and a single Yiwu major was again placed on the rolls.

sentences.zh: 秋九月辛巳，繕起太學。

translations.literal: In autumn, ninth month, day xinsi, the Imperial University was repaired and raised.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on xinsi in the ninth month, work began to rebuild the Imperial Academy.

sentences.zh: 護烏桓校尉耿曄遣兵擊鮮卑，破之。

translations.literal: Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan Geng Ye sent troops to attack the Xianbei and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan Geng Ye dispatched a force against the Xianbei and broke their army.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，於闐王遣侍子貢獻。

translations.literal: On the day dingyou, the king of Khotan sent a hostage son with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou the king of Khotan forwarded his heir as hostage together with tribute gifts.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月辛亥，詔曰：『連年灾潦，冀部尤甚。

translations.literal: In winter, eleventh month, day xinhai, an edict said: "Year after year there have been floods; the Ji region has suffered worst.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the eleventh winter month an edict declared: "Disasters from high water have struck year on year, and the Ji heartland has borne the brunt.

sentences.zh: 比蠲除實傷，贍恤窮匱，而百姓猶有弃業，流亡不絕。

translations.literal: Repeatedly We have remitted taxes according to real harm and given relief to the destitute, yet the common people still abandon their occupations, and wandering exiles do not cease.
translations.idiomatic: Time and again We have waived levies in proportion to verified damage and fed the penniless, yet farmers still abandon their fields and refugees keep streaming away.

sentences.zh: 疑郡縣用心怠惰，恩澤不宣。

translations.literal: We suspect that commanderies and counties have grown slack in spirit and failed to spread Our kindness.
translations.idiomatic: We suspect local officials have grown negligent and failed to carry Our grace to the ground.

sentences.zh: 《易》美「損上益下」，《書》稱「安民則惠」。

translations.literal: The Classic of Changes praises "taking from those above to benefit those below"; the Book of Documents says, "When the people are settled, that is kindness."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Changes celebrates "trimming the heights to enrich the lowly," and the Book of Documents declares, "Secure the people and you have shown true benevolence."

sentences.zh: 其令冀部勿收今年田租、芻稾。』

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the Ji region not collect this year's field rent or fodder tax."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the Ji region is not to collect this year's grain rent or hay levy."

sentences.zh: 十二月，日南徼外葉調國、撣國遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, beyond the frontier of Rinan, the states of Yediao and Shan sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the kingdoms of Yediao and Shan beyond Rinan's border sent missions bearing tribute.

sentences.zh: 壬申，客星出牽牛。

translations.literal: On the day renshen, a guest star appeared in the Ox mansion.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen a guest star blazed forth in the lunar lodge of the Ox.

sentences.zh: 於闐王遣侍子詣闕貢獻。

translations.literal: The king of Khotan sent a hostage son to the palace gate with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Khotan dispatched his heir to the capital to present tribute at court.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉元年春正月乙巳，立皇后梁氏。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yangjia, spring, first month, day yisi, Lady Liang was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Yangjia year, on yisi in the first spring month, a daughter of the Liang clan was invested as empress.

sentences.zh: 賜爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田三級，爵過公乘，得移與子若同產、同產子，民無名數及流民欲占著者人一級；

translations.literal: Noble rank was bestowed, two grades per person; three grades for elders of three villages, those exemplary in filial piety and fraternal duty, and promoters of agriculture; where rank exceeded gongcheng, it might be transferred to a son or to a full sibling or a full sibling's son; one grade for the unregistered and vagrants who wished to settle;
translations.idiomatic: Two steps of noble rank went to every recipient; village elders, models of filial piety and brotherly harmony, and champions of farming received three; anyone whose rank already topped gongcheng could pass the new grant to a son, a uterine brother, or that brother's son; the unregistered and migrants who chose to enroll locally received one step;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the seriously infirm, and the destitute who could not support themselves, grain, five hu each.
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely disabled, and the poor unable to survive on their own were given five hu of grain apiece.

sentences.zh: 二月，海賊曾旌等寇會稽，殺句章、鄞、鄮三縣長，攻會稽東部都尉。

translations.literal: In the second month, sea bandits such as Zeng Jing raided Kuaiji, killed the magistrates of Gouzhang, Yin, and Mao counties, and attacked the eastern Kuaiji commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month coastal raiders led by Zeng Jing struck Kuaiji, executed the magistrates of Gouzhang, Yin, and Mao, and stormed the Eastern Division commandant's headquarters.

sentences.zh: 詔緣海縣各屯兵戍。

translations.literal: It was ordered that each coastal county station troops on guard.
translations.idiomatic: Every seaboard county was ordered to post garrison troops.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，皇后謁高廟、光武廟，詔稟甘陵貧人，大小口各有差。

translations.literal: On the day dingsi, the empress performed rites at the shrines of Gaozu and Guangwu; an edict ordered relief grain for the poor of Ganling, adults and children in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi the empress worshiped at the temples of Emperor Gao and Emperor Guangwu; an edict authorized grain doles for the indigent of Ganling, scaled by household size.

sentences.zh: 京師旱。

translations.literal: In the capital there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: Drought gripped the capital.

sentences.zh: 庚申，勑郡國二千石各禱名山嶽瀆，遣大夫、謁者詣嵩高、首陽山，並祠河、洛，請雨。

translations.literal: On the day gengshen, the two thousand piculs of commanderies and kingdoms were each ordered to pray at famous mountains, great peaks, and rivers; Grandees and heralds were sent to Mount Songgao and Mount Shouyang, and the Yellow River and Luo were jointly sacrificed to, to request rain.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen every governor at two thousand piculs was told to pray at the chief peaks and streams of his jurisdiction; court grandees and heralds were dispatched to Songgao and Shouyang while joint offerings went to the Yellow River and the Luo to beg for rain.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，雩。

translations.literal: On the day wuchen, the rain prayer was performed.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen the court held the great rain ceremony.

sentences.zh: 以冀部比年水潦，民食不贍，詔案行稟貸，勸農功，賑乏絕。

translations.literal: Because the Ji region had suffered flood year after year and the people lacked food, an edict ordered inspection tours with relief loans, encouragement of farming, and aid to the utterly destitute.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Ji region had been drowned harvest after harvest and the people went hungry, an edict mandated touring inspectors, seed loans, exhortations to tillage, and relief for those without means.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，詔曰：『政失厥和，陰陽隔幷，冬鮮宿雪，春無澍雨。

translations.literal: On the day jiaxu, an edict said: "Government has missed harmony; yin and yang are blocked together; in winter there was almost no lasting snow, and in spring no soaking rain.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu an edict read: "Our rule has failed to harmonize Heaven and earth; yin and yang stand apart; winter brought hardly any settled snow, and spring no timely downpour.

sentences.zh: 分禱祈請，靡神不禜。

translations.literal: Prayers were divided and petitions sent; no spirit went unpropitiated.
translations.idiomatic: We have scattered sacrifices and prayers to every god that might listen.

sentences.zh: 深恐在所慢違「如在」之義，今遣侍中王輔等，持節分詣岱山、東海、滎陽、河、洛，盡心祈焉。』

translations.literal: We deeply fear that in the localities they have been remiss against the meaning of 'as if the spirits were present'; now We send Palace Attendants Wang Fu and others, bearing credentials, each to Mount Dai, the Eastern Sea, Xingyang, the Yellow River, and the Luo, to pray with full devotion."
translations.idiomatic: We worry that local officers have slighted the reverence owed 'as if the spirits stood above them'; Palace Attendants led by Wang Fu therefore go forth with staffs of authority to Tai Mountain, the Eastern Sea, Xingyang, the Yellow River, and the Luo to plead for rain in earnest."

sentences.zh: 三月，楊州六郡妖賊章河等寇四十九縣，殺傷長吏。

translations.literal: In the third month, in six commanderies of Yangzhou, demon bandits such as Zhang He raided forty-nine counties, killing and wounding senior officials.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month occult rebels headed by Zhang He swept six Yangzhou commanderies, hitting forty-nine counties and cutting down or wounding their magistrates.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，帝臨辟雍饗射，大赦天下，改元陽嘉。

translations.literal: On the day gengyin, the emperor attended the Circular Moat for the archery feast, a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire, and the era name was changed to Yangjia.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin the sovereign presided over the archery banquet at the Circular Moat, granted a general amnesty, and adopted the reign title Yangjia.

sentences.zh: 詔宗室絕屬籍者，一切復籍；

translations.literal: It was ordered that imperial clansmen struck from the registers all be restored;
translations.idiomatic: Members of the imperial house removed from the rolls were to be readmitted without exception;

sentences.zh: 稟冀州尤貧民，勿收今年更、租、口賦。

translations.literal: the poorest people of Ji Province were to be given relief grain, and this year's alternate service levy, land rent, and poll tax were not to be collected.
translations.idiomatic: the neediest households in Ji Province received relief grain, while the year's alternate-service fees, land tax, and head tax were waived.

sentences.zh: 夏五月戊寅，阜陵王恢薨。

translations.literal: In summer, fifth month, day wuyin, Prince Hui of Fuling died.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on wuyin in the fifth month, Prince Hui of Fuling died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，史官始作候風地動銅儀。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, the clerks of astrology for the first time constructed a bronze instrument to observe wind and detect earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh autumn month the court astronomers completed the first bronze wind gauge and seismoscope.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，以太學新成，試明經下第者補弟子，增甲、乙科員各十人。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen, because the new Imperial University was finished, those who had failed the Classics examination were enrolled as disciples, and ten places each were added to the jia and yi quotas.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen, now that the new academy stood ready, candidates who had missed the mingjing cut were admitted as student disciples, and ten additional slots were opened on both the jia and yi rosters.

sentences.zh: 除郡國耆儒九十人補郎、舍人。

translations.literal: Ninety venerable scholars of commanderies and kingdoms were appointed Gentlemen and householders.
translations.idiomatic: Ninety aged classicists from across the commanderies and kingdoms were named Gentlemen-at-large and household gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 九月，詔郡國中都官繫囚皆減死一等，亡命者贖，各有差。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, an edict ordered that capital prisoners in commanderies and kingdoms all have death sentences reduced one grade, and fugitives might ransom themselves in differing degrees.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month an edict commuted every capital convict in commandery and kingdom jails by one degree, while fugitives could buy back their guilt on a graded scale.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇遼東。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei riders invaded Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月甲申，望都、蒲陰狼殺女子九十七人，詔賜狼所殺者錢，人三千。

translations.literal: In winter, eleventh month, day jiashen, at Wangdu and Puyin wolves killed ninety-seven people; an edict granted three thousand cash to each victim.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, on jiashen in the eleventh month, wolves at Wangdu and Puyin killed ninety-seven people; the throne ordered three thousand cash paid toward each victim's funeral expenses.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，初令郡國舉孝廉，限年四十以上，諸生通章句，文吏能箋奏，乃得應選；

translations.literal: On the day xinmao, for the first time it was ordered that commanderies and kingdoms recommend filial and incorrupt men only if they were over forty, students who had mastered chapter-and-verse glosses, and clerks who could draft memorials, and only then might they answer selection;
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao a new rule required that filial-and-incorrupt nominees be at least forty, that students prove mastery of canonical segmentation, and that clerks show skill at drafting petitions before they could stand for appointment;

sentences.zh: 其有茂才異行，若顏淵、子奇，不拘年齒。

translations.literal: those who had outstanding talent and conduct, such as Yan Hui or Ziqi, were not bound by age.
translations.idiomatic: men of conspicuous genius comparable to Yan Hui or Ziqi remained exempt from the age rule.

sentences.zh: 十二月丁未，東平王敞薨。

translations.literal: On the day dingwei of the twelfth month, Prince Chang of Dongping died.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei in the twelfth month Prince Chang of Dongping died.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，復置玄菟郡屯田六部。

translations.literal: On the day gengxu, the six agricultural colonies of Xuantu Commandery were restored.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu the six garrison-farm districts of Xuantu Commandery were re-established.

sentences.zh: 閏月丁亥，令諸以詔除為郎，年四十以上課試如孝廉科者，得參廉選，歲舉一人。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, day dinghai, it was ordered that Gentlemen appointed by edict who were over forty and who passed examination on the filial-and-incorrupt model might join incorrupt selection, one man recommended per year.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month, on dinghai, Gentlemen commissioned by edict who were past forty and who cleared an examination modeled on the filial-and-incorrupt curriculum could enter the incorrupt nomination track at the rate of one per annum.

sentences.zh: 戊子，客星出天苑。

translations.literal: On the day wuzi, a guest star appeared in the Celestial Park constellation.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi a guest star shone in the Celestial Park asterism.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，詔曰：『間者以來，吏政不勤，故灾咎屢臻，盜賊多有。

translations.literal: On the day xinmao, an edict said: "Of late, officialdom has not been diligent; therefore disasters have come thickly and bandits have multiplied.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao an edict lamented: "Recently bureaucracy has slackened, so omens pile up and brigandage spreads.

sentences.zh: 退省所由，皆以選舉不實，官非其人，是以天心未得，人情多怨。

translations.literal: Withdrawing to examine the cause, it is all because recommendations have not been truthful and offices are filled with the wrong men; therefore the mind of Heaven is not obtained and human hearts harbor much resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Looking inward, the fault lies in hollow nominations and unfit appointees; Heaven's approval is withheld and popular resentment runs deep.

sentences.zh: 《書》歌股肱，《詩》刺三事。

translations.literal: The Book of Documents sings of the arms and thighs; the Classic of Poetry satirizes the three ministers.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Documents hymns the 'arms and thighs' of state, while the Classic of Poetry lampoons the three chief ministers.

sentences.zh: 今刺史、二千石之選，歸任三司。

translations.literal: Now the selection of governors and two thousand piculs is entrusted to the Three Offices.
translations.idiomatic: Henceforth the Three Offices will vet candidates for governorships and two-thousand-picul posts.

sentences.zh: 其簡序先後，精覈高下，歲月之次，文武之宜，務存厥衷。

translations.literal: Let them order precedence, examine high and low with care, the sequence of years, and the fitness of civil versus military posts, striving to hold the mean.
translations.idiomatic: They shall rank nominees, weigh merit with precision, note seniority, and balance civil against military talent, always seeking the middle way.

sentences.zh: 』庚子，恭陵百丈廡灾。

translations.literal: "On the day gengzi, the hundred-pace corridor at Gong Mausoleum caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi fire consumed the hundred-pace gallery at Emperor An's Gong mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 是歲，起西苑，修飾宮殿。

translations.literal: That year, the Western Park was begun and palace halls were refurbished.
translations.idiomatic: Work started on the Western Park and existing palace buildings were renovated.

sentences.zh: 二年春二月甲申，詔以吳郡、會稽飢荒，貸人種糧。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, second month, day jiashen, an edict, because Wu Commandery and Kuaiji were stricken by famine, loaned the people seed grain.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, on jiashen in the second spring month, an edict authorized seed-grain loans after famine struck Wu Commandery and Kuaiji.

sentences.zh: 三月，使匈奴中郎將王稠率左骨都侯等擊鮮卑，破之。

translations.literal: In the third month, the Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Wang Chou led the Left Gudu Marquis and others to attack the Xianbei and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Wang Chou, at the head of the Left Gudu Marquis and allied chiefs, struck the Xianbei and routed them.

sentences.zh: 辛酉，除京師耆儒年六十以上四十八人補郎、舍人及諸王國郎。

translations.literal: On the day xinyou, forty-eight venerable scholars of the capital aged sixty and above were appointed Gentlemen and householders and Gentlemen of the various princely kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou forty-eight capital scholars past sixty were named Gentlemen-at-large, household gentlemen, and gentlemen in the service of princely states.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，復置隴西南部都尉官。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, the office of southern division commandant in Longxi was restored.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth summer month the southern chief commandant post in Longxi was revived.

sentences.zh: 己亥，京師地震。

translations.literal: On the day jihai, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On jihai an earthquake rocked the capital.

sentences.zh: 五月庚子，詔曰：『朕以不德，統奉鴻業，無以奉順乾坤，協序陰陽，灾眚屢見，咎徵仍臻。

translations.literal: On the day gengzi of the fifth month, an edict said: "We, lacking virtue, have inherited the great enterprise and have no means to follow obediently Heaven and Earth or harmonize the sequence of yin and yang; disasters and portents appear again and again.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the fifth month an edict read: "We lack virtue yet bear the great charge; We cannot align Our rule with Heaven and earth or tune yin and yang, and omens of reproof return without end.

sentences.zh: 地動之異，發自京師，矜矜祗畏，不知所裁。

translations.literal: The prodigy of the earthquake issued from the capital; We stand in anxious fear and do not know what to decide.
translations.idiomatic: The quake began here in the capital; We tremble with awe and scarcely know what to do.

sentences.zh: 羣公卿士將何以匡輔不逮，奉荅戒異？

translations.literal: How will the assembled dukes and ministers rectify Our shortcomings and answer these warnings of prodigy?
translations.idiomatic: By what counsel will the high ministers shore up Our failures and answer these signs?

sentences.zh: 異不空設，必有所應，其各悉心直言厥咎，靡有所諱。

translations.literal: Prodigies are not set up in vain; they must correspond to something; let each exhaust his mind and speak bluntly of the fault, concealing nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven does not send warnings without cause; each of you must search his conscience and report Our errors openly, hiding nothing."

sentences.zh: 』戊午，司空王龔免。

translations.literal: "On the day wuwu, Minister of Works Wang Gong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu Minister of Works Wang Gong was removed from office.

sentences.zh: 六月辛未，太常魯國孔扶為司空。

translations.literal: On the day xinwei of the sixth month, Kong Fu of the Lu state, Minister of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in the sixth month Kong Fu of Lu, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 疏勒國獻師子、封牛。

translations.literal: The state of Shule presented lions and humped cattle.
translations.idiomatic: Shule sent lions and zebu cattle as tribute.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，洛陽地陷。

translations.literal: On the day dingchou, the ground subsided in Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou the earth opened and sank in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 是月，旱。

translations.literal: That month there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: Drought continued through that month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己未，太尉龐參免。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day jiwei, Grand Commandant Pang Can was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on jiwei in the seventh month, Grand Commandant Pang Can was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 八月己巳，大鴻臚沛國施延為太尉。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, day jisi, Shi Yan of Pei state, Grand Herald, became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month, on jisi, Grand Herald Shi Yan of Pei was named Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇代郡。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided Dai Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei horsemen struck Dai Commandery.

sentences.zh: 冬十月庚午，行禮辟雍，奏應鐘，始復黃鐘，作樂器隨月律。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day gengwu, rites were performed at the Circular Moat; the Yingzhong pitch was played, the Huangzhong pitch was restored for the first time, and instruments were made to follow the monthly scale.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, on gengwu in the tenth month, the court celebrated the Circular Moat ritual, sounded the yingzhong bell, reinstated huangzhong as the foundational pitch, and tuned new instruments to the monthly sequence.

sentences.zh: 三年春二月己丑，詔以久旱，京師諸獄無輕重皆且勿考竟，須得澍雨。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, second month, day jichou, because of prolonged drought, an edict ordered that all prisons in the capital, without distinction of severity, temporarily suspend final judgments until soaking rain should fall.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year, on jichou in the second spring month, a long drought led to an edict halting all capital trials, light or heavy, until a thorough rain should fall.

sentences.zh: 三月庚戌，益州盜賊劫質令長，殺列侯。

translations.literal: In the third month, day gengxu, bandits in Yizhou seized magistrates as hostages and killed a marquis.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, on gengxu, Yizhou outlaws took county chiefs hostage and murdered a full marquis.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙寅，車師後部司馬率後部王加特奴等掩擊匈奴，大破之，獲其季母。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day bingyin, the major for the rear division of Cheshi led Rear King Jianu and others in a surprise strike on the Xiongnu, crushing them and capturing a senior consort of their ruling house.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on bingyin in the fourth month, Cheshi's major for the rear division, with Rear King Jianu and allied riders, ambushed the Xiongnu, shattered their host, and took one of their chief consorts captive.

sentences.zh: 五月戊戌，制詔曰：『昔我太宗，丕顯之德，假於上下，儉以恤民，政致康乂。

translations.literal: On the day wuxu of the fifth month, an edict in the imperial voice said: "Of old Our Taizong, with illustrious virtue reaching above and below, practiced frugality to pity the people, and government attained peace.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu in the fifth month a throne edict recalled: "Our dynastic founder displayed radiant virtue from court to countryside, lived plainly for the people's sake, and brought the realm to good order.

sentences.zh: 朕秉事不明，政失厥道，天地譴怒，大變仍見。

translations.literal: We hold affairs unclearly; government misses the Way; Heaven and earth reprove Us with anger, and great changes still appear.
translations.idiomatic: We govern dimly; policy strays from the Way; Heaven and earth thunder their displeasure, and prodigies keep returning.

sentences.zh: 春夏連旱，寇賊彌繁，元元被害，朕甚愍之。

translations.literal: Spring and summer have brought drought in succession; bandits grow ever thicker; the common people suffer harm, and We pity them deeply.
translations.idiomatic: Drought has run from spring into summer; brigandage thickens; the people suffer, and Our heart aches for them.

sentences.zh: 嘉與海內洗心更始。

translations.literal: We rejoice with all within the seas to wash the heart and make a new beginning.
translations.idiomatic: We would join everyone under Heaven in cleansing the mind and turning a new page.

sentences.zh: 其大赦天下，自殊死以下謀反大逆諸犯不當得赦者，皆赦除之。

translations.literal: Let there be a great amnesty of the empire; from those guilty of exceptional death downward, all who in plotting rebellion and great treason would not normally merit amnesty are pardoned and remitted.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore We proclaim a sweeping amnesty: even capital crimes short of the unpardonable core of armed rebellion are cleared.

sentences.zh: 賜民年八十以上米，人一斛，肉二十斤，酒五斗；

translations.literal: To the people aged eighty and above, rice was granted, one hu per person, twenty jin of meat, and five dou of wine;
translations.idiomatic: Commoners eighty or older received one hu of rice, twenty catties of meat, and five dou of wine apiece;

sentences.zh: 九十以上加賜帛，人二匹，絮三斤。』

translations.literal: to those ninety and above, in addition silk was granted, two bolts per person, and three jin of wadding."
translations.idiomatic: those ninety or older were also given two bolts of silk and three jin of cotton wadding each."

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚戌，鐘羌寇隴西、漢陽。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day gengxu, the Zhong Qiang raided Longxi and Hanyang.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on gengxu in the seventh month, Zhong Qiang raiders hit Longxi and Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，護羌校尉馬續擊破之。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xu attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth winter month Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xu campaigned against them and won.

sentences.zh: 十一月壬寅，司徒劉崎、司空孔扶免。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, day renyin, Minister of Education Liu Qi and Minister of Works Kong Fu were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month, on renyin, Minister of Education Liu Qi and Minister of Works Kong Fu were removed.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，大司農南郡黃尚為司徒，光祿勳河東王卓為司空。

translations.literal: On the day yisi, Huang Shang of Nan commandery, Minister of Agriculture, became Minister of Education; Wang Zhuo of Hedong, Supervisor of the Household, became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi Huang Shang of Nan commandery, Minister of Agriculture, was made Minister of Education, while Supervisor of the Household Wang Zhuo of Hedong became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 丙午，武都塞上屯羌及外羌攻破屯官，驅略人畜。

translations.literal: On the day bingwu, garrison Qiang on the Wudu frontier and outer Qiang broke the garrison officers and drove off people and livestock.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu frontier Qiang stationed at Wudu, together with allied tribes without the wall, overran the garrison command and carried off people and herds.

sentences.zh: 四年春二月丙子，初聽中官得以養子為後，世襲封爵。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, second month, day bingzi, for the first time palace attendants were allowed to adopt sons as heirs and pass down enfeoffments by hereditary succession.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year, on bingzi in the second spring month, eunuchs were for the first time permitted to adopt heirs and transmit noble titles in the male line.

sentences.zh: 自去冬旱，至於是月。

translations.literal: From the previous winter's drought until this month.
translations.idiomatic: The drought that began the previous winter lasted into this month.

sentences.zh: 謁者馬賢擊鐘羌，大破之。

translations.literal: Herald Ma Xian attacked the Zhong Qiang and crushed them.
translations.idiomatic: Court herald Ma Xian struck the Zhong Qiang and shattered their force.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲子，太尉施延免。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day jiazi, Grand Commandant Shi Yan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, on jiazi in the fourth month, Grand Commandant Shi Yan was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，執金吾梁商為大將軍，前太尉龐參為太尉。

translations.literal: On the day wuyin, Liang Shang, Bearer of the Mace, became Grand General; the former Grand Commandant Pang Can became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin Bearer of the Mace Liang Shang was appointed Grand General, while former Grand Commandant Pang Can returned as Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 六月己未，梁王匡薨。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei of the sixth month, Prince Kuang of Liang died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the sixth month Prince Kuang of Liang died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己亥，濟北王登薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day jihai, Prince Deng of Jibei died.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on jihai in the seventh month, Prince Deng of Jibei died.

sentences.zh: 閏月丁亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, day dinghai, first of the month, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: The intercalary month opened on dinghai with a solar eclipse at new moon.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，烏桓寇雲中。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, the Wuhuan raided Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth winter month Wuhuan riders invaded Yunzhong.

sentences.zh: 十一月，圍度遼將軍耿曄於蘭池，發諸郡兵救之，烏桓退走。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month they besieged General Who Crosses the Liao Geng Ye at Lanchi; troops from various commanderies were raised to rescue him, and the Wuhuan withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month they surrounded General Who Crosses the Liao Geng Ye at Lanchi until relief forces from several commanderies arrived and the Wuhuan broke off the siege.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲寅，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, day jiayin, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month, on jiayin, another earthquake struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 永和元年春正月，夫餘王來朝。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yonghe, spring, first month, the king of Fuyu came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Yonghe year, during the first spring month, the king of Fuyu came to pay homage at court.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，詔曰：『朕秉政不明，灾眚屢臻。

translations.literal: On the day yimao, an edict said: "We hold government unclearly, and disasters have come thickly.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao an edict confessed: "Our administration is dim, and omens of blame keep arriving.

sentences.zh: 典籍所忌，震食為重。

translations.literal: What the canonical writings taboo, earthquake and eclipse are the weightiest.
translations.idiomatic: Among the warnings recorded in the classics, quakes and eclipses weigh heaviest."

sentences.zh: 今日變方遠，地搖京師，咎徵不虛，必有所應。

translations.literal: Today the change is still distant, yet the earth shook the capital; the sign of blame is not empty and must correspond to something.
translations.idiomatic: Though the omen has not yet run its full course, the earth has quaked beneath the capital; such a reproof from Heaven cannot be meaningless.

sentences.zh: 群公百僚其各上封事，指陳得失，靡有所諱。

translations.literal: Let the assembled dukes and the hundred officials each submit sealed memorials, pointing out gain and loss, concealing nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Every high minister and official is to forward a sealed statement naming what has gone right and what wrong, hiding nothing.

sentences.zh: 』己巳，宗祀明堂，登靈臺，改元永和，大赦天下。

translations.literal: "On the day jisi, ancestral rites were performed at the Bright Hall, the Spirit Terrace was ascended, the era name was changed to Yonghe, and a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi the sovereign sacrificed at the Bright Hall, climbed the Spirit Terrace, adopted the reign title Yonghe, and declared a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，偃師蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, locusts struck Yanshi.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed Yanshi in the seventh autumn month.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丁亥，承福殿火，帝避御雲臺。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day dinghai, the Hall of Received Blessings caught fire; the emperor withdrew his court to the Cloud Terrace.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai in the tenth winter month fire destroyed the Hall of Received Blessings; the court removed to the Cloud Terrace.

sentences.zh: 十一月丙子，太尉龐參罷。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, day bingzi, Grand Commandant Pang Can was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi in the eleventh month Grand Commandant Pang Can was removed.

sentences.zh: 十二月，象林蠻夷叛。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the barbarians of Xianglin rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the non-Chinese peoples of Xianglin rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，以前司空王龔為太尉。

translations.literal: On the day yisi, the former Minister of Works Wang Gong was appointed Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi former Minister of Works Wang Gong was named Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，武陵蠻叛，圍充縣，又寇夷道。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month, the Wuling Man rebelled, besieged Chong County, and also raided Yidao.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year's first spring month the Wuling tribesmen rebelled, surrounded Chong county, and struck Yidao as well.

sentences.zh: 二月，廣漢屬國都尉擊破白馬羌。

translations.literal: In the second month, the chief commandant of the dependent state of Guanghan defeated the White Horse Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month the chief commandant of Guanghan's dependent state broke the White Horse Qiang.

sentences.zh: 武陵太守李進擊叛蠻，破之。

translations.literal: Wuling Governor Li Jin attacked the rebel Man and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Li Jin of Wuling campaigned against the rebel tribes and won.

sentences.zh: 三月辛亥，北海王翼薨。

translations.literal: In the third month, day xinhai, Prince Yi of Beihai died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the third month Prince Yi of Beihai died.

sentences.zh: 乙卯，司空王卓薨。

translations.literal: On the day yimao, Minister of Works Wang Zhuo died.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao Minister of Works Wang Zhuo died.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，光祿勳馮翊郭虔為司空。

translations.literal: On the day dingchou, Guo Qian of Fanyi, Supervisor of the Household, became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou Guo Qian of Fanyi, Supervisor of the Household, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙申，京師地震。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day bingshen, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On bingshen in the fourth summer month an earthquake rocked the capital.

sentences.zh: 五月，日南叛蠻攻郡府。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, rebel Man of Rinan attacked the commandery headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month insurgent tribesmen of Rinan stormed the commandery yamen.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，九真、交阯二郡兵反。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, the troops of Jiuquan and Jiaozhi commanderies mutinied.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh autumn month the garrisons of Jiuquan and Jiaozhi turned against their officers.

sentences.zh: 八月庚子，熒惑犯南斗。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, day gengzi, Mars intruded on the Southern Dipper.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the eighth month Mars entered the lunar lodge of the Southern Dipper.

sentences.zh: 江夏盜賊殺邾長。

translations.literal: Bandits in Jiangxia killed the magistrate of Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Outlaws in Jiangxia murdered the magistrate of Zhu.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲申，行幸長安，所過鰥、寡、孤、獨、貧不能自存者賜粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day jiashen, an imperial progress was made to Chang'an; widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, and the destitute who could not support themselves along the route were granted grain, five hu each.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the tenth winter month the emperor traveled to Chang'an; every indigent widower, widow, orphan, or childless person encountered on the way received five hu of grain.

sentences.zh: 庚子，幸未央宮，會三輔郡守、都尉及官屬，勞賜作樂。

translations.literal: On the day gengzi, he visited Weiyang Palace, met the governors and chief commandants of the three metropolitan circuits and their subordinates, feasted them, and granted rewards with music.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi he attended Weiyang Palace, summoned the governors and chief commandants of the three capital circuits and their staffs, and held a banquet with music and gifts.

sentences.zh: 十一月丙午，祠高廟。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, day bingwu, sacrifice was offered at the shrine of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu in the eleventh month he sacrificed at Emperor Gao's temple.

sentences.zh: 丁未，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: On the day dingwei, he then performed the great rites at the eleven mausolea.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei he completed the grand offerings at the eleven imperial tombs.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，京師地震。

translations.literal: On the day dingmao, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao an earthquake struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 十二月乙亥，至自長安。

translations.literal: On the day yihai of the twelfth month, he returned from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the twelfth month the court returned from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 三年春二月乙亥，京師及金城、隴西地震，二郡山岸崩，地陷。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, second month, day yihai, the capital, Jincheng, and Longxi were shaken by earthquake; mountain cliffs collapsed in the two commanderies and the earth subsided.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year, on yihai in the second spring month, earthquakes hit the capital, Jincheng, and Longxi; cliffs gave way in the two western commanderies and the ground opened.

sentences.zh: 戊子，太白犯熒惑。

translations.literal: On the day wuzi, Venus intruded on Mars.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi Venus occulted or passed Mars.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，九江賊蔡伯流寇郡界，及廣陵，殺江都長。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, the bandit Cai Boliu of Jiujiang raided the commandery border and reached Guangling, killing the magistrate of Jiangdu.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth summer month Jiujiang rebel Cai Boliu plundered the commandery marches, pushed into Guangling, and slew the magistrate of Jiangdu.

sentences.zh: 戊戌，遣光祿大夫案行金城、隴西，賜壓死者年七歲以上錢，人二千；

translations.literal: On the day wuxu, Palace Attendants were sent to inspect Jincheng and Longxi and to grant two thousand cash to each person crushed to death aged seven and above;
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu court envoys toured Jincheng and Longxi, paying two thousand cash for every victim of seven years or older killed in the collapse;

sentences.zh: 一家皆被害，為收斂之。

translations.literal: where a whole household had perished, the authorities were to bury them.
translations.idiomatic: where an entire household had been lost, officials arranged the burials.

sentences.zh: 除今年田租，尤甚者勿收口賦。

translations.literal: This year's field rent was remitted, and where harm was worst the poll tax was not collected.
translations.idiomatic: Land tax for the year was waived, and in the hardest-hit districts the head tax was not levied.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丙戌，濟北王多薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day bingxu, Prince Duo of Jibei died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu in the seventh autumn month Prince Duo of Jibei died.

sentences.zh: 八月己未，司徒黃尚免。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, day jiwei, Minister of Education Huang Shang was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the eighth month Minister of Education Huang Shang was removed.

sentences.zh: 九月己酉，光祿勳長沙劉壽為司徒。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, day jiyou, Liu Shou of Changsha, Supervisor of the Household, became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou in the ninth month Liu Shou of Changsha, Supervisor of the Household, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，令大將軍、三公各舉故刺史、二千石及見令、長、郎、謁者、四府掾屬剛毅武猛有謀謨任將帥者各二人，特進、卿、校尉各一人。

translations.literal: On the day bingxu, it was ordered that the Grand General and the Three Dukes each recommend two men—former governors, two thousand piculs, or incumbent magistrates, chiefs, Gentlemen, heralds, or clerks of the four offices—who were resolute, martial, and bold, possessed of strategy fit for command; each Excellency, chief commandant, and colonel was to recommend one.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu an edict required the Grand General and the Three Dukes each to nominate two officers—among ex-governors, former two-thousand-picul officials, sitting magistrates, chiefs, Gentlemen, heralds, or staff of the four ministerial bureaus—who were stern, brave, and clever enough for field command, while each chief minister, chief commandant, and colonel was to put forward one such man.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，燒當羌寇金城，護羌校尉馬賢擊破之，羌遂相招而叛。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, the Shao Dang Qiang raided Jincheng; Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xian defeated them, whereupon the Qiang called one another and rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth winter month the Shao Dang Qiang struck Jincheng; Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xian repulsed them, but the tribes then rallied in wider revolt.

sentences.zh: 十二月戊戌朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, day wuxu, first of the month, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: The twelfth month opened on wuxu with a solar eclipse at new moon.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月庚辰，中常侍張逵、蘧政、楊定等有罪誅，連及弘農太守張鳳、安平相楊告，下獄死。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, first month, day gengchen, the palace attendants Zhang Kui, Qu Zheng, Yang Ding, and others were executed for their crimes, implicating Governor of Hongnong Zhang Feng and Chancellor of Anping Yang Gao, who were imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year, on gengchen in the first spring month, eunuchs Zhang Kui, Qu Zheng, and Yang Ding were put to death for conspiracy; Governor Zhang Feng of Hongnong and Chancellor Yang Gao of Anping were caught in the net, jailed, and died there.

sentences.zh: 三月乙亥，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the third month, day yihai, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the third month an earthquake shook the capital.

sentences.zh: 夏四月癸卯，護羌校尉馬賢討燒當羌，大破之。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day guimao, Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xian campaigned against the Shao Dang Qiang and crushed them.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao in the fourth summer month Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ma Xian attacked the Shao Dang Qiang and routed them utterly.

sentences.zh: 戊午，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the day wuwu, a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵及粟、帛各有差。

translations.literal: The people were granted noble rank as well as grain and silk in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners received steps of noble rank plus grain and silk in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 五月戊辰，封故濟北惠王壽子安為濟北王。

translations.literal: On the day wuchen, An, son of the late Prince Hui of Jibei, was enfeoffed as Prince of Jibei.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen An, son of the late Prince Hui of Jibei, was invested as Prince of Jibei.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，太原郡旱，民庶流冗。

translations.literal: In autumn, eighth month, Taiyuan Commandery suffered drought; the people drifted in destitution.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth autumn month Taiyuan was parched by drought and the populace scattered as refugees.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，遣光祿大夫案行稟貸，除更賦。

translations.literal: On the day guichou, Palace Attendants were sent to inspect with relief loans, and alternate-service levies were remitted.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou envoys toured with seed and relief grain, while fees for hired substitute corvée were waived.

sentences.zh: 冬十月戊午，校獵上林苑，歷函谷關而還。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day wuwu, a hunt was held in the Shanglin Park, passing Hangu Pass on the return.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu in the tenth winter month the emperor hunted in Shanglin Park and came back by way of Hangu Pass.

sentences.zh: 十一月丙寅，幸廣成苑。

translations.literal: On the day bingyin of the eleventh month, he visited the Guangcheng Park.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin in the eleventh month he traveled to Guangcheng Park.

sentences.zh: 五年春二月戊申，京師地震。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, spring, second month, day wushen, the capital was shaken by an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year, on wushen in the second spring month, the capital quaked.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚子，中山王弘薨。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day gengzi, Prince Hong of Zhongshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the fourth summer month Prince Hong of Zhongshan died.

sentences.zh: 南匈奴左部句龍大人吾斯、車紐等叛，圍美稷。

translations.literal: The great chiefs Wusi and Cheniu of the left division of the Southern Xiongnu rebelled and besieged Meiji.
translations.idiomatic: Wusi and Cheniu, chiefs of the Southern Xiongnu's left wing, mutinied and surrounded the headquarters at Meiji.

sentences.zh: 五月，度遼將軍馬續討吾斯、車紐，破之，使匈奴中郎將陳龜迫殺南單于。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, General Who Crosses the Liao Ma Xu campaigned against Wusi and Cheniu and defeated them; Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Chen Gui pressed the Southern Chanyu to his death.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month General Who Crosses the Liao Ma Xu struck Wusi and Cheniu and broke their power; Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Chen Gui then hounded the Southern Chanyu until he perished.

sentences.zh: 己亚晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jisi, the last day of the month, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: The month ended on jisi with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 且凍羌寇三輔，殺令長。

translations.literal: The Qie Dong Qiang raided the three metropolitan circuits and killed magistrates.
translations.idiomatic: The Qie Dong Qiang invaded the capital region and murdered county chiefs.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，令死罪以下及亡命贖，各有差。

translations.literal: On the day dingchou, it was ordered that from death sentences downward and for fugitives ransom be allowed in differing degrees.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou an edict permitted ransoms for all crimes down from capital offenses, as well as for fugitives, on a graded scale.

sentences.zh: 九月，令扶風、漢陽築隴道塢三百所，置屯兵。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, it was ordered that Fengyi and Hanyang build three hundred fortified posts along the Long road and station garrison troops.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Fengyi and Hanyang were told to erect three hundred blockhouses along the Long mountain road and man them with soldiers.

sentences.zh: 辛未，太尉王龔罷。

translations.literal: On the day xinwei, Grand Commandant Wang Gong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei Grand Commandant Wang Gong was removed.

sentences.zh: 且凍羌寇武都，燒隴關。

translations.literal: The Qie Dong Qiang raided Wudu and burned Long Pass.
translations.idiomatic: The Qie Dong Qiang struck Wudu and set fire to Long Pass.

sentences.zh: 壬午，太常桓焉為太尉。

translations.literal: On the day renwu, Huan Yan, Minister of Ceremonies, became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu Huan Yan, Minister of Ceremonies, was appointed Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，徙西河郡居離石，上郡居夏陽，朔方居五原。

translations.literal: On the day dinghai, Xihe Commandery was shifted to lodge at Lishi, Shang Commandery at Xiayang, and Shuofang at Wuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai the seat of Xihe was moved to Lishi, that of Shang to Xiayang, and Shuofang's administration to Wuyuan.

sentences.zh: 句龍吾斯等東引烏桓，西收羌胡，寇上郡，立車紐為單于。

translations.literal: Goulong Wusi and others drew the Wuhuan eastward, gathered Qiang and Hu westward, raided Shang Commandery, and set up Cheniu as Chanyu.
