translations.idiomatic: Wusi of the Goulong line pulled Wuhuan bands east, recruited Qiang and Hu from the west, invaded Shang Commandery, and enthroned Cheniu as Chanyu.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月辛巳，遣使匈奴中郎將張耽擊破之，車紐降。

translations.literal: In winter, eleventh month, day xinsi, Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Zhang Dan was sent and defeated them; Cheniu surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the eleventh winter month Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Zhang Dan attacked them and won; Cheniu submitted.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月丙子，征西將軍馬賢與且凍羌戰於射姑山，賢軍敗沒，安定太守郭璜下獄死。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, spring, first month, day bingzi, General on the West Ma Xian fought the Qie Dong Qiang at Mount Shegu; Xian's army was defeated and annihilated, and Governor of Anding Guo Huang was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, on bingzi in the first spring month, General on the West Ma Xian met the Qie Dong Qiang at Shegu Mountain; his host was shattered and he perished, while Anding Governor Guo Huang was jailed and died.

sentences.zh: 詔貸王、侯國租一歲。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the rent of princely kingdoms be loaned for one year.
translations.idiomatic: An edict deferred collection of one year's rent from princely fiefs.

sentences.zh: 閏月，鞏唐羌寇隴西，遂及三輔。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, the Gong Tang Qiang raided Longxi and then reached the three metropolitan circuits.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month the Gong Tang Qiang hit Longxi and pushed into the capital region.

sentences.zh: 二月丁巳，有星孛於營室。

translations.literal: In the second month, day dingsi, a broom star appeared in Encampment.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the second month a comet blazed in the Encampment lunar lodge.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲午，詔假民有貲者戶錢一千。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day jiawu, an edict ordered a loan of one thousand cash per household to families possessing assets.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the seventh autumn month an edict advanced one thousand cash per propertied household as a loan.

sentences.zh: 八月丙辰，大將軍梁商薨；

translations.literal: In the eighth month, day bingchen, Grand General Liang Shang died;
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the eighth month Grand General Liang Shang died;

sentences.zh: 壬戌，河南尹梁冀為大將軍。

translations.literal: on the day renxu, Liang Ji, Intendant of Henan, became Grand General.
translations.idiomatic: on renxu Liang Ji, Intendant of Henan, succeeded him as Grand General.

sentences.zh: 九月，諸種羌寇武威。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, various Qiang tribes raided Wuwei.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month allied Qiang bands invaded Wuwei.

sentences.zh: 辛亥晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month xinhai, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: The month closed on xinhai with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸丑，徙安定居扶風，北地居馮翔。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day guichou, Anding was shifted to lodge in Fufeng, Beidi in Fengyi.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the tenth winter month the population of Anding commandery was resettled in Fufeng, and that of Beidi commandery in Fengyi.

sentences.zh: 十一月庚子，以執金吾張喬行車騎將軍事，將兵屯三輔。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, day gengzi, Bearer of the Mace Zhang Qiao was appointed to act as General of Chariots and Cavalry and lead troops to encamp in the three metropolitan circuits.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the eleventh month Bearer of the Mace Zhang Qiao was commissioned acting General of Chariots and Cavalry and sent with an army to garrison the capital circuits.

sentences.zh: 漢安元年春正月癸巳，宗祀明堂，大赦天下，改元漢安。

translations.literal: In the first year of Han'an, spring, first month, day guisi, ancestral rites were performed at the Bright Hall, a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire, and the era name was changed to Han'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Han'an year, on guisi in the first spring month, sacrifice was offered at the Bright Hall, a general amnesty was granted, and the reign title became Han'an.

sentences.zh: 二月丙辰，詔大將軍、公、卿舉賢良方正、能探賾索隱者各一人。

translations.literal: In the second month, day bingchen, an edict ordered the Grand General, the dukes, and the ministers each to recommend one worthy and upright man able to plumb the hidden and search out the subtle.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the second month the Grand General and the high ministers were each told to nominate one candidate of integrity able to probe recondite matters.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，始置承華廄。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, the Chenghua stables were first established.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh autumn month the Chenghua imperial stables were founded for the first time.

sentences.zh: 八月，南匈奴左部大人句龍吾斯與薁鞬臺耆等反叛。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, the great chief Goulong Wusi of the Southern Xiongnu left wing, with Yu Jian Taigi and others, rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Goulong Wusi, chief of the Southern Xiongnu's eastern wing, rose with Yu Jian Taigi and allied chiefs.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，遣侍中杜喬，光祿大夫周舉，守光祿大夫郭遵、馮羨、欒巴、張綱、周栩、劉班等八人分行州、郡，班宣風化，舉實臧否。

translations.literal: On the day dingmao, Palace Attendants Du Qiao and Zhou Ju, Acting Palace Attendants Guo Zun, Feng Xian, Luan Ba, Zhang Gang, Zhou Xu, and Liu Ban—eight men in all—were sent separately through the provinces and commanderies to proclaim moral transformation and report truthfully on good and ill.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao eight inspectors—Palace Attendants Du Qiao and Zhou Ju, and acting Palace Attendants Guo Zun, Feng Xian, Luan Ba, Zhang Gang, Zhou Xu, and Liu Ban—fanned out through the provinces and commanderies to spread the court's teaching and report honestly on local conduct.

sentences.zh: 九月庚寅，廣陵盜賊張嬰等寇郡縣。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, day gengyin, the bandit Zhang Ying of Guangling and others raided commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the ninth month Guangling rebel Zhang Ying and his followers pillaged towns across the region.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛未，太尉桓焉、司徒劉壽免。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day xinwei, Grand Commandant Huan Yan and Minister of Education Liu Shou were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in the tenth winter month Grand Commandant Huan Yan and Minister of Education Liu Shou were removed.

sentences.zh: 甲戌，行車騎將軍張喬罷。

translations.literal: On the day jiaxu, Acting General of Chariots and Cavalry Zhang Qiao was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu Acting General of Chariots and Cavalry Zhang Qiao was relieved of command.

sentences.zh: 十一月壬午，司隸校尉趙峻為太尉，大司農胡廣為司徒。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, day renwu, Zhao Jun, Metropolitan Governor, became Grand Commandant, and Hu Guang, Minister of Agriculture, became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu in the eleventh month Metropolitan Governor Zhao Jun was named Grand Commandant and Minister of Agriculture Hu Guang became Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，詔大將軍、三公選武猛試用有效驗任為將校者各一人。

translations.literal: On the day guimao, an edict ordered the Grand General and the Three Dukes each to select one man of martial vigor whose trial appointment had proven effective and who might serve as general or colonel.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao the Grand General and the Three Dukes were each told to pick one officer of proven military mettle from a probationary post fit for command as general or colonel.

sentences.zh: 是歲，廣陵賊張嬰等詣太守張綱降。

translations.literal: That year, the bandit Zhang Ying of Guangling and others went to Governor Zhang Gang and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The same year Zhang Ying of Guangling and his band submitted to Governor Zhang Gang.

sentences.zh: 二年春二月丙辰，鄯善國遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, second month, day bingchen, the state of Shanshan sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, on bingchen in the second spring month, Shanshan sent a tribute mission.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚戌，護羌校尉趙沖與漢陽太守張貢擊燒何羌於參欒，破之。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day gengxu, Colonel Protecting the Qiang Zhao Chong and Governor of Hanyang Zhang Gong attacked the Shao He Qiang at Canluan and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu in the fourth summer month Colonel Protecting the Qiang Zhao Chong and Hanyang Governor Zhang Gong struck the Shao He Qiang at Canluan and routed them.

sentences.zh: 六月乙丑，熒惑犯鎮星。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, day yichou, Mars intruded on Saturn.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou in the sixth month Mars approached Saturn.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，立南匈奴守義王兜樓儲為南單于。

translations.literal: On the day bingyin, the Southern Xiongnu's Defender of the Faith, Prince Dou Lou Chu, was established as Southern Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin Dou Lou Chu, the Southern Xiongnu's "prince who guards righteousness," was enthroned as Southern Chanyu.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛丑，令郡國中都官繫囚殊死以下出縑贖，各有差；

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, day xinchou, it was ordered that capital prisoners in commanderies and kingdoms from exceptional death downward be released on payment of silk in differing amounts;
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the tenth winter month convicts in commandery and capital jails from capital crimes downward might buy release with graded payments of silk;

sentences.zh: 其不能入贖者，遣詣臨羌縣居作二歲。

translations.literal: those unable to pay ransom were to be sent to Linqiang County to labor for two years.
translations.idiomatic: those who could not raise the ransom were sent to Linqiang county for two years of hard labor.

sentences.zh: 甲辰，減百官奉。

translations.literal: On the day jiachen, the salaries of the hundred officials were reduced.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen official stipends were cut.

sentences.zh: 丙午，禁沽酒，又貸王、侯國租一歲。

translations.literal: On the day bingwu, the sale of wine was forbidden, and the rent of princely kingdoms was again loaned for one year.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu wine was banned from the market and princely rents were deferred another year.

sentences.zh: 閏王，趙沖擊燒當羌於阿陽，破之。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, Zhao Chong attacked the Shao Dang Qiang at Ayang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month Zhao Chong campaigned against the Shao Dang Qiang at Ayang and won.

sentences.zh: 十一月，使匈奴中郎將馬寔遣人刺殺句龍吾斯。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Ma Shi sent men who assassinated Goulong Wusi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Ma Shi had Goulong Wusi murdered by agents.

sentences.zh: 十二月，楊、徐盜賊攻燒城寺，殺略吏民。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, bandits of Yang and Xu attacked and burned walled towns and official compounds, killing and plundering officials and people.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month rebels in Yang and Xu torched county seats and government offices, slaughtering and looting officials and commoners.

sentences.zh: 是歲，涼州地百八十震。

translations.literal: That year, the earth in Liang Province shook one hundred eighty times.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Province suffered one hundred eighty earthquakes in the course of the year.

sentences.zh: 建康元年正月辛丑，詔曰：『隴西、漢陽、張掖、北地、武威、武都，自去年九月已來，地百八十震，山谷坼裂，壞敗城寺，殺害民庶。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jiankang, first month, day xinchou, an edict said: "Longxi, Hanyang, Zhangye, Beidi, Wuwei, and Wudu have, since the ninth month of last year, been shaken by the earth one hundred eighty times; mountains and valleys have split; city walls and monasteries have been ruined; common people have been killed.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Jiankang year, on xinchou in the first month, an edict read: "Since last year's ninth month Longxi, Hanyang, Zhangye, Beidi, Wuwei, and Wudu have endured one hundred eighty tremors; ridges have cracked open, towns and temples toppled, and the people crushed.

sentences.zh: 夷狄叛逆，賦役重數，內外怨曠，惟咎嘆息。

translations.literal: The barbarians rebel; taxes and corvée are piled in repeated layers; inside and outside there is resentment and emptiness; We sigh only in blame.
translations.idiomatic: Tribesmen rise in revolt while levies and labor multiply; court and countryside seethe with grievance, and We can only groan at Our fault.

sentences.zh: 其遣光祿大夫案行，宣暢恩澤，惠此下民，勿為煩擾。』

translations.literal: Let Palace Attendants be sent to inspect, to clarify and spread kindness, to favor these lower people, and not to vex and disturb them."
translations.idiomatic: Envoys of rank will tour the region, carry Our grace to the humblest households, and must not harass them with needless fuss."

sentences.zh: 三月庚子，沛王廣薨。

translations.literal: In the third month, day gengzi, Prince Guang of Pei died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the third month Prince Guang of Pei died.

sentences.zh: 領護羌校尉衛琚追討叛羌，破之。

translations.literal: Acting Colonel Protecting the Qiang Wei Ju pursued and attacked the rebel Qiang and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Acting Colonel Protecting the Qiang Wei Ju chased the rebel tribes and broke their host.

sentences.zh: 南郡、江夏盜賊寇掠城邑，州郡討平之。

translations.literal: Bandits in Nan Commandery and Jiangxia raided and plundered walled towns; the province and commanderies campaigned and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Brigands in Nan and Jiangxia looted cities until provincial forces stamped them out.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，使匈奴中郎將馬寔擊南匈奴左部，破之，於是胡羌、烏桓悉詣寔降。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Ma Shi attacked the left wing of the Southern Xiongnu and defeated them; thereupon Hu, Qiang, and Wuhuan all came to Shi and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth summer month Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Ma Shi struck the Southern Xiongnu's left division and broke it; Hu, Qiang, and Wuhuan bands then flocked to his camp and submitted.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，立皇子炳為皇太子，改年建康，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the day xinsi, the imperial son Bing was established as heir apparent, the era name was changed to Jiankang, and a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi the emperor's son Bing was named heir apparent, the reign title became Jiankang, and a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 賜人爵各有差。

translations.literal: Noble rank was bestowed on the people in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: The people received graded grants of noble rank.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丙午，清河王延平薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day bingwu, Prince Yanping of Qinghe died.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu in the seventh autumn month Prince Yanping of Qinghe died.

sentences.zh: 八月，楊、徐盜賊范容、周生等寇掠城邑，遣御史中丞馮赦督州郡兵討之。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, bandits Fan Rong and Zhou Sheng of Yang and Xu raided walled towns; Imperial Secretary Feng She was dispatched to supervise provincial and commandery troops against them.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Yang and Xu rebels Fan Rong and Zhou Sheng pillaged towns; Imperial Secretary Feng She took charge of provincial armies sent to suppress them.

sentences.zh: 庚午，帝崩於玉堂前殿，時年三十。

translations.literal: On the day gengwu, the emperor died in the forecourt of the Jade Hall; he was thirty sui of age.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu the sovereign expired in the Jade Hall antechamber, aged thirty by Chinese reckoning.

sentences.zh: 遺詔無起寢廟，斂以故服，珠玉玩好皆不得下。

translations.literal: In his testamentary edict he ordered no resting mausoleum shrine raised, that he be shrouded in his accustomed robes, and that pearls, jade, and playthings not be interred with him.
translations.idiomatic: His deathbed will forbid erecting a separate temple-tomb, direct burial in his everyday garments, and bar pearls, jade, and luxuries from the coffin.

sentences.zh: 論曰：古之人君，離幽放而反國祚者有矣，莫不矯鑒前違，審識情偽，無忘在外之憂，故能中興其業。

translations.literal: The essayist says: Among rulers of old who were cast into darkness and exile yet recovered the throne, there have been such; none failed to take warning from earlier errors, discern truth from pretense, and remember the peril of life outside the court—thus they could revive their enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: ancient sovereigns who fell into disgrace yet won back the throne invariably learned from past mistakes, judged sincerity from guile, and never forgot the danger of exile—only so could they restore the dynasty.

sentences.zh: 觀夫順朝之政，殆不然乎？

translations.literal: Observing the government of the Shun court, was it perhaps not so?
translations.idiomatic: Yet when one weighs the reign called Shun, does the pattern apply at all?

sentences.zh: 何其傚僻之多與？

translations.literal: How very many were the instances of perverse imitation?
translations.idiomatic: How often did the court instead copy the wrong examples?

sentences.zh: 孝沖皇帝諱炳，順帝之子也。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaochong, taboo name Bing, was a son of Emperor Shun.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor Xiaochong, personal name Bing, was Emperor Shun's son.

sentences.zh: 母曰虞貴人。

translations.literal: His mother was Honored Lady Yu.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was the Honored Lady Yu.

sentences.zh: 建康元年立為皇太子，其年八月庚午，即皇帝位，年二歲。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jiankang he was established as heir apparent; in that year, eighth month, day gengwu, he assumed the imperial position; he was two sui of age.
translations.idiomatic: Named heir apparent in the first Jiankang year, he ascended the throne on gengwu in the eighth month of that same year at the age of two.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was elevated to empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后臨朝。

translations.literal: The empress dowager held court.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager assumed regency and presided over court.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，以太尉趙峻為太傅；

translations.literal: On the day dingchou, Grand Commandant Zhao Jun was made Grand Tutor;
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou Grand Commandant Zhao Jun became Grand Tutor;

sentences.zh: 大司農李固為太尉，參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Li Gu, Minister of Agriculture, became Grand Commandant and participated in supervising the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Agriculture Li Gu succeeded him as Grand Commandant with concurrent oversight of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 九月丙午，葬孝順皇帝於憲陵，廟曰敬宗。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, day bingwu, Emperor Shun was buried at Xian Mausoleum; his temple name was Jingzong.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu in the ninth month the late Emperor Shun was interred at Xian Mausoleum and given the temple name Jingzong.

sentences.zh: 是日，京師及太原、鴈門地震，三郡水涌土裂。

translations.literal: That day, the capital, Taiyuan, and Yanmen were shaken by earthquake; in the three commanderies water burst from the earth and the ground split.
translations.idiomatic: The same day earthquakes rocked the capital, Taiyuan, and Yanmen; in all three commanderies springs erupted through fissures in the soil.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，詔三公、特進、侯、卿、校尉，舉賢良方正、幽逸修道之士各一人，百僚皆上封事。

translations.literal: On the day gengxu, an edict ordered the Three Dukes, nobles of special advancement, marquises, ministers, and chief commandants each to recommend one worthy and upright man or recluse devoted to moral cultivation, and the hundred officials to submit sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu the high ministers, specially advanced nobles, marquises, chief ministers, and chief commandants were each told to nominate one candidate—either a worthy and upright scholar or a recluse known for self-cultivation—while every official filed sealed advice.

sentences.zh: 己未，九江太守丘騰有罪，下獄死。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, Jiujiang Governor Qiu Teng was guilty of crime, was imprisoned, and died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei Jiujiang Governor Qiu Teng was convicted, jailed, and died there.

sentences.zh: 楊州刺史尹耀、九江太守鄧顯討賊范容等於歷陽，軍敗，耀、顯為賊所歿。

translations.literal: Yang Province Inspector Yin Yao and Jiujiang Governor Deng Xian campaigned against bandit Fan Rong and others at Liyang; the army was defeated, and Yao and Xian perished at the hands of the bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector Yin Yao of Yang Province and Jiujiang Governor Deng Xian attacked Fan Rong's band at Liyang but were routed; both officers fell to the rebels.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，日南蠻夷攻燒城邑，交阯刺史夏方招誘降之。

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, the southern barbarians of Rinan attacked and burned walled towns; Jiaozhi Inspector Xia Fang summoned and induced them to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth winter month tribal peoples of Rinan torched towns until Jiaozhi Inspector Xia Fang talked them into submission.

sentences.zh: 壬申，常山王儀薨。

translations.literal: On the day renshen, Prince Yi of Changshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen Prince Yi of Changshan died.

sentences.zh: 己卯，零陵太守劉康坐殺無辜，下獄死。

translations.literal: On the day jimao, Lingling Governor Liu Kang was guilty of killing the innocent, was imprisoned, and died.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao Lingling Governor Liu Kang was condemned for executing innocents, imprisoned, and died.

sentences.zh: 十一月，九江盜賊徐鳳、馬勉等稱『無上將軍』，攻燒城邑。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Jiujiang bandits Xu Feng and Ma Mian and others styled themselves "Supreme General" and attacked and burned walled towns.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Jiujiang rebels Xu Feng and Ma Mian took the title Supreme General and burned their way through several towns.

sentences.zh: 己酉，令郡國中都官繫囚減死一等，徙邊；

translations.literal: On the day jiyou, it was ordered that capital prisoners in commanderies and kingdoms have death sentences reduced one grade and be banished to the frontier;
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou capital convicts in commandery and kingdom jails had capital sentences commuted one degree and were sent to the frontier;

sentences.zh: 謀反大逆，不用此令。

translations.literal: for plotting rebellion and great treason this order did not apply.
translations.idiomatic: the order did not cover treason and armed rebellion.

sentences.zh: 十二月，九江賊黃虎等攻合肥。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, Jiujiang bandit Huang Hu and others attacked Hefei.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Jiujiang raider Huang Hu and his followers struck Hefei.

sentences.zh: 是歲，群盜發憲陵。

translations.literal: That year, a crowd of bandits broke into Xian Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: That year tomb robbers broke open Emperor Shun's Xian mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 護羌校尉趙沖追擊叛羌於鹯陰河，戰歿。

translations.literal: Colonel Protecting the Qiang Zhao Chong pursued rebel Qiang at the Zhanyin River and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Protecting the Qiang Zhao Chong chased rebel Qiang to the Zhanyin River and was killed in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 永憙元年春正月戊戌，帝崩於玉堂前殿，年三歲。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongxi, spring, first month, day wuxu, the emperor died in the forecourt of the Jade Hall; he was three sui of age.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Yongxi era, on wuxu in the first spring month, the child emperor died in the Jade Hall antechamber, aged three.

sentences.zh: 清河王蒜徵至京師。

translations.literal: Prince Suan of Qinghe was summoned to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Suan of Qinghe was called to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 孝質皇帝諱纘，肅宗玄孫。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaozhi, taboo name Zuan, was a great-great-grandson of Emperor Suzong.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor Xiaozhi, personal name Zuan, was a fourth-generation descendant of Emperor Zhang, temple name Suzong.

sentences.zh: 曾祖父千乘貞王伉，祖父樂安夷王寵，父勃海孝王鴻，母陳夫人。

translations.literal: His great-grandfather was Prince Kang of Qiancheng, taboo Kang; his grandfather was Prince Chong of Le'an, posthumous title Yi; his father was Prince Hong of Bohai, posthumous title Xiao; his mother was Lady Chen.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather was Prince Kang of Qiancheng, personal name Kang; his grandfather was Prince Chong of Le'an, posthumously called the Mild Prince; his father was Prince Hong of Bohai, posthumously called the Filial Prince; his mother was Lady Chen.

sentences.zh: 沖帝不豫，大將軍梁冀徴帝到洛陽都亭。

translations.literal: When Emperor Chong was gravely ill, Grand General Liang Ji summoned the prince to the metropolitan relay lodge at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: While Emperor Chong lay dying, Grand General Liang Ji had the boy prince brought to the capital relay inn.

sentences.zh: 及沖帝崩，皇太后與冀定策禁中，丙辰，使冀持節，以王青蓋車迎帝入南宮。

translations.literal: When Emperor Chong died, the empress dowager and Liang Ji settled the succession within the palace; on the day bingchen, Ji was sent bearing credentials to welcome the emperor in a royal carriage with green canopy into the Southern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: After the young emperor's death the empress dowager and Liang Ji chose the heir in camera; on bingchen Liang Ji, staff in hand, escorted the prince in the green-canopied royal carriage into the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，封為建平侯，其日即皇帝位，年八歲。

translations.literal: On the day dingsi, he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Jianping, and the same day he assumed the imperial position; he was eight sui of age.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi he received the marquisate of Jianping and the same day ascended the throne at the age of eight.

sentences.zh: 己未，葬孝沖皇帝於懷陵。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, Emperor Xiaochong was buried at Huai Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei Emperor Chong was interred at Huai Mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 廣陵賊張嬰等復反，攻殺堂邑、江都長。

translations.literal: Guangling bandit Zhang Ying and others rebelled again, attacked and killed the magistrates of Tangyi and Jiangdu.
translations.idiomatic: Guangling outlaw Zhang Ying rose again, stormed Tangyi and Jiangdu, and slew both magistrates.

sentences.zh: 九江賊徐鳳等攻殺曲陽、東城長。

translations.literal: Jiujiang bandit Xu Feng and others attacked and killed the magistrates of Quyang and Dongcheng.
translations.idiomatic: Jiujiang rebels under Xu Feng killed the chiefs of Quyang and Dongcheng.

sentences.zh: 甲申，謁高廟。

translations.literal: On the day jiashen, he performed rites at the shrine of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen he sacrificed at Emperor Gao's temple.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，謁光武廟。

translations.literal: On the day yiyou, he performed rites at the shrine of Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou he worshiped at Emperor Guangwu's temple.

sentences.zh: 二月，豫章太守虞續坐贓，下獄死。

translations.literal: In the second month, Yuzhang Governor Yu Xu was guilty of embezzlement, was imprisoned, and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Yuzhang Governor Yu Xu was convicted of graft, jailed, and died.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On the day yiyou, a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 賜人爵及粟、帛各有差。

translations.literal: The people were granted noble rank as well as grain and silk in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: The people received noble rank plus grain and silk in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 還王侯所削戶、邑。

translations.literal: To kings and marquises were restored the households and fiefs that had been stripped from them.
translations.idiomatic: Princes and marquises had confiscated households and towns returned to their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 彭城王道薨。

translations.literal: Prince Dao of Pengcheng died.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Dao of Pengcheng died.

sentences.zh: 叛羌詣左馮翊梁並降。

translations.literal: Rebel Qiang went to Zuo Fengyi and submitted to Liang Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Defector Qiang bands came to Liang Bing, chief of Zuo Fengyi, and surrendered.

sentences.zh: 三月，九江賊馬勉稱『黃帝』。

translations.literal: In the third month, Jiujiang bandit Ma Mian styled himself "Yellow Emperor."
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Jiujiang rebel Ma Mian proclaimed himself the Yellow Emperor.

sentences.zh: 九江都尉滕撫討馬勉、范容、周生，大破斬之。

translations.literal: Jiujiang Commandant Teng Fu campaigned against Ma Mian, Fan Rong, and Zhou Sheng, crushed them, and beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: Jiujiang Commandant Teng Fu attacked Ma Mian, Fan Rong, and Zhou Sheng, shattered their forces, and took their heads.

sentences.zh: 夏四月壬申，雩。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day renshen, the rain prayer was performed.
translations.idiomatic: On renshen in the fourth summer month the court held the great rain ceremony.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，濟北王安薨。

translations.literal: On the day gengchen, Prince An of Jibei died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen Prince An of Jibei died.

sentences.zh: 丹陽賊陸宮等圍城，燒亭寺，丹陽太守江漢擊破之。

translations.literal: Danyang bandits such as Lu Gong besieged a city, burned post stations and yamen; Danyang Governor Jiang Han attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Danyang raiders led by Lu Gong ringed a county seat and torched postal stations and offices until Governor Jiang Han defeated them.

sentences.zh: 五月甲午，詔曰：『朕以不德，託母天下，布政不明，每失厥中。

translations.literal: On the day jiawu of the fifth month, an edict said: "We lack virtue; relying on Our mother We bear the realm, yet Our government is unclear and We constantly miss the mean.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the fifth month an edict read: "We are without merit; entrusted to Our mother We rule the world, yet Our policies stay clouded and We miss the golden mean.

sentences.zh: 自春涉夏，大旱炎赫，憂心京京，故得禱祈明祀，冀蒙潤澤。

translations.literal: From spring into summer there has been great drought and blazing heat; Our heart is anxious; therefore We have prayed at the bright shrines, hoping to receive moisture.
translations.idiomatic: From spring through summer drought has scorched the land; Our mind is never at rest, hence We have sacrificed at the great shrines, begging for rain.

sentences.zh: 前雖得雨，而宿麥頗傷；

translations.literal: Although rain came earlier, the winter wheat was much harmed;
translations.idiomatic: Earlier showers came too late for the winter wheat, which suffered badly;

sentences.zh: 比日陰雲，還復開霽。

translations.literal: in recent days clouds gathered but then cleared again.
translations.idiomatic: lately gray skies have broken into clear sun again.

sentences.zh: 寤寐永歎，重懷慘結。

translations.literal: Awake or asleep We sigh without end; grief knots Our breast anew.
translations.idiomatic: We toss and sigh without cease; sorrow ties Our heart in knots.

sentences.zh: 將二千石、令、長不崇寬和。

translations.literal: Is it that the two thousand piculs, magistrates, and chiefs do not honor breadth and harmony?
translations.idiomatic: Perhaps the governors, magistrates, and chiefs have abandoned the way of leniency.

sentences.zh: 暴刻之為乎？

translations.literal: Is it the doing of violence and harshness?
translations.idiomatic: Or are cruelty and grinding exactions to blame?

sentences.zh: 其令中都官繫囚罪非殊死考未竟者，一切任出，以須立秋。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that capital prisoners whose crimes are not capital and whose examinations are not yet complete all be released on recognizance until the Beginning of Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore capital jail inmates not sentenced to death whose cases are unfinished shall be freed on bond until the qi Beginning of Autumn.

sentences.zh: 郡國有名山大澤能興雲雨者，二千石長吏各絜齊請禱，謁誠盡禮。

translations.literal: In commanderies and kingdoms that possess famous mountains and great marshes able to summon clouds and rain, let each two-thousand-picul chief fast, pray, and perform the rites with utmost sincerity.
translations.idiomatic: Where a commandery boasts peaks and lakes that draw rain, its governor must fast, pray, and observe the full ritual with utter sincerity.

sentences.zh: 又兵役連年，死亡流離，或支骸不斂，或停棺莫收，朕甚愍焉。

translations.literal: Moreover, military service has continued year after year; there has been death and wandering; some limbs lie unburied, some coffins stand uncollected; We pity this deeply.
translations.idiomatic: War has dragged on for years, scattering the dead and the living; bones lie in the open and coffins wait in the lanes—We grieve for them all.

sentences.zh: 昔文王葬枯骨，人賴其德。

translations.literal: In olden days King Wen buried dry bones, and the people relied on his virtue.
translations.idiomatic: King Wen of old gathered up dry bones for burial, and the realm trusted his virtue.

sentences.zh: 今遣使者案行，若無家屬及貧無資者，隨宜賜恤，以慰孤魂。』

translations.literal: Now We send envoys to inspect; where there are no family members or the poor lack means, grant relief as appropriate, to comfort orphaned souls."
translations.idiomatic: We now dispatch inspectors: where kin are absent or the poor cannot afford rites, give aid as fit, that wandering souls may find peace."

sentences.zh: 是月，下邳人謝安應募擊徐鳳等，斬之。

translations.literal: That month, Xie An of Xiapi answered the levy and attacked Xu Feng and others, beheading them.
translations.idiomatic: That month Xie An of Xiapi, answering the recruitment call, struck Xu Feng's band and cut off their heads.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，詔曰：『孝殤皇帝雖不永休祚，而即位踰年，君臣禮成。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen, an edict said: "Emperor Shang, though his blessing was not long, had been on the throne more than a full year; the rites between ruler and minister were complete.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen an edict stated: "Emperor Shang, though his allotted reign was short, had completed more than a full year on the throne; the courtesies owed sovereign and subject were satisfied.

sentences.zh: 孝安皇帝承襲統業，而前世遂令恭陵在康陵之上，先後相踰，失其次序，非所以奉宗廟之重，垂無窮之制。

translations.literal: Emperor An inherited the unified enterprise, yet earlier generations had placed Gong Mausoleum above Kang Mausoleum, reversing the proper sequence of earlier and later; this was not the way to honor the weight of the ancestral temples or to hand down an endless model.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor An continued the unified line, yet a prior reign had sited Gong mausoleum above Kang, inverting the proper order of senior and junior—hardly the way to honor the ancestral shrines or fix precedent for ages to come.

sentences.zh: 昔定公追正順祀，《春秋》善之。

translations.literal: In olden days Duke Ding corrected the order of sacrifice, and the 《Spring and Autumn Annals》 praised him.
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Ding of Lu rectified the sequence of mortuary worship, the 《Spring and Autumn Annals》 approved the deed.

sentences.zh: 其令恭陵次康陵，憲陵次恭陵，以序親秩，為萬世法。』

translations.literal: Let Gong Mausoleum be ranked after Kang Mausoleum, and Xian Mausoleum after Gong Mausoleum, thereby ordering the degrees of kinship and establishing a model for ten thousand generations."
translations.idiomatic: Therefore Gong tomb shall stand junior to Kang, and Xian junior to Gong, restoring the ladder of kinship as a norm for all time."

sentences.zh: 六月，鮮卑寇代郡。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, the Xianbei raided Dai Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Xianbei horsemen invaded Dai Commandery.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚寅，阜陵王代薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month, day gengyin, Prince Dai of Fuling died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the seventh autumn month Prince Dai of Fuling died.

sentences.zh: 廬江盜賊攻尋陽，又攻盱台，滕撫遣司馬王章擊破之。

translations.literal: Lujiang bandits attacked Xunyang and also attacked Yutai; Teng Fu sent Major Wang Zhang to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: Lujiang rebels stormed Xunyang and Yutai until Teng Fu's major Wang Zhang routed them.

sentences.zh: 九月庚戌，太傅趙峻薨。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, day gengxu, Grand Tutor Zhao Jun died.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu in the ninth month Grand Tutor Zhao Jun died.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月己丑，南陽太守韓昭坐贓下獄死。

translations.literal: In winter, eleventh month, day jichou, Nanyang Governor Han Zhao was guilty of embezzlement, was imprisoned, and died.
translations.idiomatic: On jichou in the eleventh winter month Nanyang Governor Han Zhao was convicted of corruption and died in prison.

sentences.zh: 丙午，中郎將滕撫擊廣陵賊張嬰，破之。

translations.literal: On the day bingwu, Central Army commander Teng Fu attacked Guangling bandit Zhang Ying and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: On bingwu Central Army commander Teng Fu campaigned against Zhang Ying of Guangling and broke his force.

sentences.zh: 丁未，中郎將趙序坐事弃市。

translations.literal: On the day dingwei, Central Army commander Zhao Xu, guilty of an offense, was executed in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei Central Army commander Zhao Xu was executed in the marketplace for misconduct.

sentences.zh: 歷陽賊華孟自稱『黑帝』，攻殺九江太守楊岑，滕撫率諸將擊孟等，大破斬之。

translations.literal: Liyang bandit Hua Meng styled himself "Black Emperor," attacked and killed Jiujiang Governor Yang Cen; Teng Fu led the generals against Meng and others, crushed them, and beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: Hua Meng of Liyang called himself the Black Emperor, murdered Jiujiang Governor Yang Cen, then fell to Teng Fu's generals, who destroyed his army and took his head.

sentences.zh: 本初元年春正月丙申，詔曰：『昔堯命四子，以欽天道，《鴻範》九疇，休咎有象。

translations.literal: In the first year of Benchu, spring, first month, day bingshen, an edict said: "Of old Yao charged the Four Masters to reverence the Way of Heaven; the nine divisions of the 《Hong Fan》 set out images of blessing and blame.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Benchu year, on bingshen in the first spring month, an edict recalled: "Yao once appointed four ministers to align the realm with Heaven's pattern; the nine divisions of the 《Hong Fan》 showed how fortune and reproof mirror conduct.

sentences.zh: 夫瑞以和降，異因逆感，禁微應大，前聖所重。

translations.literal: Auspicious omens descend through harmony; prodigies arise from perverse resonance; checking the subtle answers the great—this is what former sages prized.
translations.idiomatic: Good portents arrive when harmony rules; ill omens track defiance of Heaven; curbing small excesses averts great disaster—so the sages taught.

sentences.zh: 頃者，州郡輕慢憲防，競逞殘暴，造設科條，陷入無罪。

translations.literal: Of late, provinces and commanderies have treated the statutes lightly, vied in cruelty, devised regulations, and trapped the guiltless.
translations.idiomatic: Yet lately governors have sneered at the code, outdone one another in brutality, and invented clauses to ensnare the innocent.

sentences.zh: 或以喜怒驅逐長吏，恩阿所私，罰枉仇隙，至令守闕訴訟，前後不絕。

translations.literal: Some from favor or anger have driven out senior officials, shown partiality to private friends, punished wrongly for old grudges, until people besieged the palace gate suing in an endless stream.
translations.idiomatic: Some remove magistrates on a whim, show favor to cronies, and punish foes without justice, until litigants block the palace gate without end.

sentences.zh: 送故迎新，人離其害，怨氣傷和，以致灾眚。

translations.literal: Seeing off the old and welcoming the new, the people suffer the harm of separation; resentful qi injures harmony and brings disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Constant turnover of officials tears people from their homes; malice poisons harmony and invites calamity.

sentences.zh: 《書》云：「明德慎罰。

translations.literal: The 《Book of Documents》 says: "Clarify bright virtue and be careful with punishments.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Book of Documents》 urges rulers to clarify bright virtue and handle punishments with scrupulous care.

sentences.zh: 」方春東作，育微敬始。

translations.literal: "Now spring opens the eastern labors; it is the season to nurture the subtle and reverence beginnings.
translations.idiomatic: Spring calls forth the work of the east; it is time to foster small beginnings and honor renewal.

sentences.zh: 其勑有司，罪非殊死，且勿案驗，以崇在寬。』

translations.literal: Let the responsible offices be ordered: crimes not capital are for the moment not to be investigated, thereby honoring the ideal of breadth."
translations.idiomatic: Tell every bureau: suspend inquests for non-capital cases for now, that the policy of mercy may prevail."

sentences.zh: 壬子，廣陵太守王喜坐討賊逗留，下獄死。

translations.literal: On the day renzi, Guangling Governor Wang Xi, guilty of delaying while campaigning against bandits, was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi Guangling Governor Wang Xi was jailed and died for dawdling on anti-bandit duty.

sentences.zh: 二月庚辰，詔曰：『九江、廣陵二郡數離寇害，殘夷最甚。

translations.literal: In the second month, day gengchen, an edict said: "The two commanderies of Jiujiang and Guangling have repeatedly suffered bandit ravages; the slaughter has been worst there.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen in the second month an edict read: "Jiujiang and Guangling have been ravaged again and again; nowhere is the slaughter worse.

sentences.zh: 生者失其資業。

translations.literal: The living have lost their livelihoods.
translations.idiomatic: Survivors have lost their farms and trades.

sentences.zh: 死者委屍原野。

translations.literal: The dead lie abandoned on the plain.
translations.idiomatic: Corpses lie unburied on the open field.

sentences.zh: 昔之為政，一物不得其所，若己為之，況我元元，嬰此困毒。

translations.literal: Of old, in governing, if a single creature missed its proper place, the ruler felt as if he had caused it; how much more Our common people, caught in this bitter poison.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient kings held that if one thing were out of joint they bore the blame themselves; how much more must We answer for Our people trapped in such agony.

sentences.zh: 方春戒節，賑濟乏戹，掩骼埋胔之時。

translations.literal: Now is the season of spring discipline, the time to relieve destitution and gather bones for burial.
translations.idiomatic: Spring's discipline is the season to feed the hungry and gather the dead for burial.

sentences.zh: 其調比郡見穀，出稟窮弱，收葬枯骸，務加埋卹，以稱朕意。』

translations.literal: Let grain be levied from neighboring commanderies' available stores, issued in relief to the weak, and dry bones collected for burial; strive to add burial succor, to match Our mind."
translations.idiomatic: Requisition grain from neighboring counties, issue it to the feeble, and bury the bleaching bones with care—do this in earnest, that Our intent may be honored."

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚辰，令郡國舉明經，年五十以上、七十以上詣太學。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month, day gengchen, it was ordered that commanderies and kingdoms nominate men learned in the Classics between fifty and seventy sui of age to proceed to the Imperial University.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen in the fourth summer month commanderies and kingdoms were told to send Classicists aged from fifty to seventy sui to study at the Imperial University.

sentences.zh: 自大將軍至六百石，皆遣子受業，歲滿課試，以高第五人補郎中，次五人太子舍人。

translations.literal: From the Grand General down to six hundred piculs, all were to send sons to receive instruction; at the year's end they were examined by test; the five highest were appointed court gentlemen, the next five gentlemen of the heir apparent's household.
translations.idiomatic: Every official from the Grand General down to six hundred piculs had to enroll sons for coursework; at year's end the top five examinees became gentlemen of the court, the next five gentlemen-in-waiting of the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 又千石、六百石、四府掾屬、三署郎、四姓小侯先能通經者，各令隨家法，其高第者上名牒，當以次賞進。

translations.literal: Moreover, those at one thousand and six hundred piculs, clerks of the four ministerial bureaus, gentlemen of the three departments, and junior marquises of the four great clans who had already mastered the classics were each to follow their family's scholastic tradition; those with highest scores were to submit name lists and would be rewarded and advanced in order.
translations.idiomatic: Likewise thousand- and six-hundred-picul officers, staff of the four offices, gentlemen of the three corps, and four-clan cadet marquises who already knew the canon were to study their household school; top graduates filed nominal rolls for graded promotion.

sentences.zh: 五月庚寅，徙樂安王為勃海王。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, day gengyin, the Prince of Le'an was transferred to be Prince of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the fifth month the Prince of Le'an was reassigned as Prince of Bohai.

sentences.zh: 海水溢。

translations.literal: Seawater overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The sea breached its banks and flooded the coast.

sentences.zh: 戊申，使謁者案行，收葬樂安、北海人為水所漂沒死者，又稟給貧羸。

translations.literal: On the day wushen, Heralds were sent to inspect, gather for burial the people of Le'an and Beihai who had been swept away and drowned by floodwaters, and also grant relief to the poor and wasted.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen court heralds toured the flood zone, collecting the bodies of Le'an and Beihai victims the waters had carried off for decent burial, while grain went to the destitute and starving.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，太白犯熒惑。

translations.literal: On the day gengxu, Venus intruded on Mars.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu Venus drew near Mars in the sky.

sentences.zh: 六月丁巳，大赦天下，賜民爵及粟、帛各有差。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, day dingsi, a great amnesty was proclaimed for the empire; the people were granted noble rank as well as grain and silk in differing amounts.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the sixth month a general amnesty was declared, together with graded gifts of noble rank, grain, and silk to the populace.

sentences.zh: 閏月甲申，大將軍梁冀潛行鴆弒，帝崩於玉堂前殿，年九歲。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, day jiashen, Grand General Liang Ji secretly administered poisoned ale; the emperor died in the forecourt of the Jade Hall; he was nine sui of age.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the intercalary month Grand General Liang Ji had the boy emperor murdered with poisoned wine in the Jade Hall antechamber; he was nine years old.

sentences.zh: 丁亥，太尉李固免。

translations.literal: On the day dinghai, Grand Commandant Li Gu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai Grand Commandant Li Gu was removed from office.

sentences.zh: 戊子，司徒胡廣為太尉，司空趙戒為司徒，與梁冀參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On the day wuzi, Minister of Education Hu Guang became Grand Commandant, Minister of Works Zhao Jie became Minister of Education, and together with Liang Ji they participated in supervising the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi Hu Guang rose from Minister of Education to Grand Commandant, Zhao Jie from Minister of Works to Minister of Education, both joining Liang Ji in oversight of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 太僕袁湯為司空。

translations.literal: Yuan Tang, Grand Coachman, became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Coachman Yuan Tang was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：孝順初立，時髦允集。

translations.literal: The encomium says: When Emperor Shun first took the throne, the finest talents of the age flocked to court.
translations.idiomatic: The summation reads: at Emperor Shun's accession the realm's ablest men gathered about him.

sentences.zh: 匪砥匪革，終淪嬖習。

translations.literal: They were neither honed like whetstones nor reformed like tanned hide; in the end they sank into the habits of petty favorites.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they neither sharpened their character nor changed their ways, and the court slid at last into the clique of bedchamber favorites.

sentences.zh: 保阿傳土，后家世及。

translations.literal: Nurse-attendants transmitted the altars; generation after generation the empress's house succeeded to power.
translations.idiomatic: Women of the inner palace and their patrons passed the throne hand to hand, while the Liang consort clan inherited authority reign after reign.

sentences.zh: 沖夭未識，質弒以聰。

translations.literal: Chong died young before he could know the world; Zhi was murdered on account of his cleverness.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Chong was cut off in infancy before he could understand governance; Emperor Zhi was assassinated because his sharp mind threatened his handlers.

sentences.zh: 陵折在運，天緒三終。

translations.literal: The imperial tombs rose and fell with the turning of fate; Heaven's thread ran out three times in a row.
translations.idiomatic: The fate of the imperial tombs mirrored the turn of the times; three successive boy emperors saw the heavenly succession brought to a sudden end.
